Local organizations like the Afghanistan Research and Evaluation Unit (AREU) continue limited research operations. UNAMA (United Nations Assistance Mission in Afghanistan) monitors human rights and governance.

Afghanistan's formal corporate sector is largely non-functional under current conditions. International engagement occurs through multilateral development organizations and humanitarian agencies. The Afghanistan Investment Authority was established to attract foreign investment but is currently non-operational.

FAO (Food and Agriculture Organization) — maintains agricultural programs in Afghanistan focused on food security and sustainable farming. UNODC (United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime) — publishes annual Afghanistan Opium Survey tracking cultivation area, yield, and farm-gate prices. World Bank Afghanistan Reconstruction Trust Fund (ARTF) — the largest single source of development funding for Afghanistan, currently operating in limited capacity under sanções constraints.

Note: Afghanistan under Taliban rule has no elected legislature or representative democratic institutions. International engagement channels include: UN Security Council sanções regime (Resolution 2255), which allows humanitarian exemptions. The Doha process for political dialogue. Afghan diaspora organizations in Europe, North America, and Australia. International development partners coordinate through the Geneva Conference on Afghanistan.

AL.s: candidato à UE country with small open economia ($27B PIB). Oil production (1.04% rents) centered in Patos-Marinzë field. Services-dominant (48.7% PIB) with significant hydropower potential (95%+ of electricity from hydro).

sectors.w: Albania's oil sector is small and concentrated in aging onshore fields. As EU accession negotiations require ambiental acquis alignment, the interesse sobreposto between oil producers and government revenue enfraquece — EU ambiental standards make marginal fields unprofitable.
bp: EU accession ambiental alignment eliminates subsidies for marginal oil production. Quando Albania must meet EU ambiental standards, o interesse sobreposto em continued extraction from aging fields (many dating to communist era) disappears — cleanup costs exceed production revenue.

Implementar EU-aligned ambiental monitoring and emissões disclosure. Albania's EITI (Extractive Industries Transparency Initiative) membership provides a framework — extending to emissões makes W visible.
Não é possível quebrar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report ambiental contamination at oil production sites to AKM (National Environment Agency)
Document health impacts on communities near oil fields — report to Institute of Public Health
Use Albania's denunciante protection law (Law 60/2016) for reporting ambiental violations
Albpetrol: Desenvolver decommissioning and site remediation plan for aging oil fields
Investir em energia renovável diversification — Albania has significant untapped solar and wind potential
Adotar EITI enhanced reporting standards including ambiental metrics
AKM: Aplicar ambiental remediation requirements at legacy oil production sites
AKBN: Require ambiental bonds for all new exploration licenses
ERE (Energy Regulatory Entity): Accelerate energia renovável permitting
Contacte o seu member of the Kuvendi (Albanian Parliament) para exigir ambiental remediation of legacy oil sites
Support accelerated EU accession negotiations contingent on ambiental acquis compliance
Advocate for inclusion of ambiental cleanup costs in Albania's EITI reporting
Pressionar por Energy Community Treaty enforcement on energia renovável targets
i: AKM (Agjencia Kombëtare e Mjedisit / National Environment Agency) — responsible for ambiental monitoring, permitting, and enforcement. AKM conducts ambiental impact assessments for energy projects. AKBN (Agjencia Kombëtare e Burimeve Natyrore / National Agency of Natural Resources) — regulates oil and gas exploration and production licenses. Law 60/2016 'On Whistleblowing and Whistleblower Protection' — Albania's denunciante protection statute, adopted as part of EU accession reforms, providing protection against retaliation for reporting violations of law.

Albpetrol — Albania's estatal oil company, managing legacy fields from the communist era. Albpetrol's operations include some of Europe's most contaminated industrial sites. Bankers Petroleum (now Geo-Jade Petroleum, Chinese-owned) — the largest oil producer in Albania, operating the Patos-Marinzë field. EITI (Extractive Industries Transparency Initiative) — Albania has been an EITI member since 2013, publishing annual reports on oil and mineração revenues.

ERE (Enti Rregullator i Energjisë / Energy Regulatory Entity) — Albania's independent regulador de energia, responsible for licensing, tariff-setting, and market regulation in the electricity and natural gas sectors. Ministry of Infrastructure and Energy — oversees national energy policy including the National Energy Strategy 2030, which targets significant energia renovável expansion.

Kuvendi i Shqipërisë (Parliament of Albania) — unicameral legislature with 140 members elected by proportional representation. Citizens can contact their deputies through parlament.al. The Komisioni për Veprimtarinë Prodhuese (Committee on Production Activities) handles energy and ambiental legislation. Energy Community Treaty — Albania is a member of the Energy Community, which extends EU energy and ambiental acquis to Southeast European countries. Non-compliance can result in dispute settlement proceedings.

AM.s: Small landlocked economia ($26B PIB) with significant mineração sector (min=6.79% of PIB, primarily copper-molybdenum at Kajaran and Sotk gold mine). Services-dominant (62% PIB). Geopolitical constraints from Nagorno-Karabakh conflict and closed borders with Turkey and Azerbaijan.

sectors.w: Armenia's mineração sector generates significant water pollution (Debed and Voghji rivers) and tailings dam risks. Quando downstream water quality data is published alongside mine production revenue, W se torna computável from bilateral transactions.
bp: Mandate ambiental bonds and rehabilitation deposits that fully internalize mine closure costs. Quando mineração companies must pre-fund ambiental remediation, o interesse sobreposto em extraction without cleanup enfraquece — marginal mines become unprofitable.

Mandatory real-time water quality monitoring downstream of all mineração operations, published openly. Armenia's EITI membership provides a disclosure framework — extending to ambiental monitoring data makes W visible from production data.

Parcialmente quebrável. Open-pit mineração inherently disturbs landscapes and generates acid mine drainage. However, modern tailings management and water treatment can reduce the magnitude of dependência do sistema.
a: Report ambiental violations at mine sites to the Environmental and Mining Inspection Body
Document water pollution downstream of tailings facilities — report to the Ministry of Environment
Use Armenia's denunciante protection provisions under the Anti-Corruption Strategy for reporting
Implementar international best practice tailings management (Global Industry Standard on Tailings Management)
Adotar EITI enhanced reporting including ambiental impact metrics
Commission independent water quality assessments for all operations
Ministry of Environment: Fortalecer ambiental impact assessment requirements for new mineração licenses
EMIB: Increase inspection frequency and enforce penalties for water pollution violations
Ministry of Territorial Administration: Require community consent processes for new mine approvals
Contacte o seu member of the Azgayin Zhoghov (National Assembly) para exigir mandatory ambiental bonds for all mineração operations
Support strengthening of EITI reporting requirements to include ambiental impact data
Advocate for EU-Armenia CEPA (Comprehensive and Enhanced Partnership Agreement) enforcement on ambiental standards
Pressionar por international arbitration standards that include ambiental remediation in mineração investment disputes
i: Environmental and Mining Inspection Body (EMIB) — the Armenian government agency responsible for monitoring compliance with ambiental regulations at mineração operations, conducting inspections, and imposing sanções. Ministry of Environment — oversees ambiental policy, issues ambiental impact assessment approvals, and sets water quality standards. Armenia's Anti-Corruption Council — established under the Prime Minister's office, provides channels for reporting corruption and misconduct in the mineração sector.

Zangezur Copper-Molybdenum Combine (ZCMC) — Armenia's largest mineração company, operating the Kajaran copper-molybdenum mine. Lydian International / Lydian Armenia — operator of the controversial Amulsar gold mine project. GeoProMining — Russian-owned company operating gold mines in Armenia. EITI — Armenia became an EITI candidate country in 2017, requiring disclosure of mineração revenues and contracts.

Ministry of Territorial Administration and Infrastructure — oversees mineração policy and licensing. Competition Protection Commission — monitors market concentration in the mineração sector. Armenian EITI Multi-Stakeholder Group — includes government, industry, and sociedade civil representatives overseeing extractive sector transparência.

Azgayin Zhoghov (National Assembly of Armenia) — unicameral parliament with 107 members. Citizens can contact their representatives through parliament.am. Armenia's 2018 revolution established stronger democratic institutions. EU-Armenia CEPA (Comprehensive and Enhanced Partnership Agreement) — signed in 2017, includes provisions on ambiental protection and sustainable development that Armenia has committed to implement.

AO.s: Major African oil producer with 29.28% combined oil and gas rents ($29.6B). Sonangol (state oil company) dominates the economia. Agriculture (22.1% PIB) is largely subsistence. Post-civil war recovery continues with high inequality.

sectors.w: Angola's oil production is declining from mature deepwater fields. As extraction costs rise and global demand plateaus, the interesse sobreposto between Sonangol and international partners (TotalEnergies, BP, ExxonMobil) enfraquece — declining reserves make diversification economically rational.
bp: Redirect Sonangol investment from deepwater exploration to energia renovável and agricultural modernization. Angola's solar potential is enormous (2,500+ kWh/m²/year). Quando renewable returns exceed marginal barrel returns from declining fields, the interesse sobreposto breaks.

Implementar EITI-standard revenue transparência extended to per-block ambiental impact data. Angola rejoined EITI in 2020 — extending disclosure to emissões and ambiental costs makes W computable per production unit.
Não é possível quebrar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report ambiental violations at oil facilities to MIREMPET (Ministry of Mineral Resources, Petroleum and Gas)
Document community health impacts near production facilities through local NGOs
Report financial irregularities in Sonangol operations — Angola's anti-corruption framework (Lei 3/20) provides limited protections
Sonangol: Desenvolver strategic diversification plan with binding energia renovável investment targets
International partners (TotalEnergies, BP): Implementar Paris-aligned emissões reduction plans for Angolan operations
Adotar TCFD-aligned climate disclosure for all production sharing agreements
ANPG: Require ambiental impact assessments with community consultation for all new production blocks
Ministry of Environment: Fortalecer enforcement of ambiental regulations in the petroleum sector
Banco Nacional de Angola: Integrate climate risk into financial supervision of oil-dependent lending
Contacte o seu member of the Assembleia Nacional para exigir publication of per-block ambiental impact data for all oil operations
Support EITI enhanced reporting with ambiental metrics for Angola
Advogar através de the African Union for continental energia renovável investment targets
Pressionar por Paris Agreement compliance monitoring through UNFCCC mechanisms
i: MIREMPET (Ministério dos Recursos Minerais, Petróleo e Gás) — Angola's ministry overseeing oil and gas operations, licensing, and regulation. ANPG (Agência Nacional de Petróleo, Gás e Biocombustíveis) — the national petroleum regulator, created in 2019 to replace Sonangol's dual role as regulator and operator. Lei 3/20 (Law on Public Probity) — Angola's anti-corruption law establishing asset declaration requirements and conflicts of interest rules for public officials. Whistleblower protection remains weak.

Sonangol E.P. — Angola's state oil company and the country's largest enterprise. Sonangol holds equity in all oil production sharing agreements and manages the state's petroleum interests. The company underwent restructuring in 2019, separating its regulatory functions to ANPG. TotalEnergies, BP, ExxonMobil, Eni — major international oil companies operating deepwater blocks offshore Angola.

ANPG (Agência Nacional de Petróleo, Gás e Biocombustíveis) — established in 2019 as the independent petroleum regulator, responsible for managing exploration and production contracts, monitoring compliance with license terms, and administering bidding rounds. Ministério do Ambiente — Angola's environment ministry, responsible for ambiental policy, impact assessments, and pollution control.

Assembleia Nacional (National Assembly of Angola) — unicameral legislature with 220 members. Angola's electoral system uses party-list proportional representation. The most recent elections were held in 2022. Citizens can engage through assembleia.ao. Angola is a presidential republic with significant executive power. EITI — Angola was suspended from EITI in 2017 for insufficient progress but successfully reapplied and was readmitted in 2020, committing to enhanced transparência standards.

AR.s: G20 economia ($638.4B PIB) with massive Vaca Muerta shale formation (second-largest shale gas, fourth-largest shale oil reserves globally). Agricultural powerhouse (soy, beef) with 5.8% ag PIB share but outsized ambiental footprint. Chronic macroeconomic instability.

sectors.w: Argentina's Vaca Muerta development requires massive capital investment ($10B+/year) and government subsidies (Plan Gas). Removing subsidies breaks the interesse sobreposto — without price guarantees, many Vaca Muerta wells are marginal at global market prices.
bp: Phase out Plan Gas subsidies and redirect capital to energia renovável. Argentina has world-class wind resources in Patagonia and solar in the northwest. Quando renewable electricity undercuts subsidized gas, the interesse sobreposto between producers and government in continued fossil expansion breaks.

Mandatory per-well emissões disclosure including methane venting and flaring. Argentina's Secretaría de Energía already collects production data — extending to emissões makes W computable from production statistics.
Não é possível quebrar by policy alone. Shale gas extraction and combustion release CO₂ and methane by physical process.
a: Report ambiental violations at Vaca Muerta operations to the provincial autoridade ambiental (Secretaría de Ambiente de Neuquén)
Document water contamination from fracking operations — report to AIC (Autoridad Interjurisdiccional de las Cuencas)
Report safety violations to the Secretaría de Trabajo
YPF: Desenvolver Paris-aligned transition plan with binding emissões reduction targets for Vaca Muerta operations
Implementar TCFD-aligned climate disclosure across all operations
Investir em CCUS (carbon capture, utilization, and storage) pilot projects for gas processing facilities
Secretaría de Energía: Publish per-well emissões data for all Vaca Muerta operations
SAyDS: Fortalecer federal ambiental standards for unconventional hydrocarbon extraction
ENARGAS: Reformar natural gas tariff structure to eliminate implicit fossil fuel subsidies
Contacte o seu Diputado Nacional or Senador para exigir a timeline for fossil fuel subsidy phase-out and transparent per-well emissões reporting
Fortalecer Argentina's NDC — current targets are considered insufficient by Climate Action Tracker
Leverage Mercosur-EU trade agreement ambiental provisions to align energy transition commitments
Support South American energia renovável integration through regional grid interconnection
i: Secretaría de Ambiente de Neuquén — Neuquén province's autoridade ambiental, responsible for ambiental permitting and monitoring of oil and gas operations in the Vaca Muerta formation. AIC (Autoridad Interjurisdiccional de las Cuencas de los ríos Limay, Neuquén y Negro) — the inter-jurisdictional water authority managing the river basins affected by Vaca Muerta operations. COFEMA (Consejo Federal de Medio Ambiente) — federal-provincial ambiental coordination body. Argentina lacks comprehensive federal denunciante protection legislation, though provincial laws vary.

YPF S.A. — Argentina's largest energy company (51% estatal), the dominant operator in Vaca Muerta. YPF's strategic decisions on Vaca Muerta development drive national energy policy. Pan American Energy, Vista Energy, TotalEnergies — other major operators in Vaca Muerta. Plan Gas.Ar — the Argentine government's gas production incentive program, guaranteeing minimum prices for domestic gas production to stimulate Vaca Muerta development.

Secretaría de Energía — Argentina's energy secretariat, responsible for energy policy, production monitoring, and regulatory oversight. SAyDS (Secretaría de Ambiente y Desarrollo Sustentable) — the federal environment secretariat, responsible for ambiental policy and mudança climática. ENARGAS (Ente Nacional Regulador del Gas) — the national gas regulatory authority, overseeing gas transport, distribution, and tariff regulation.

Congreso de la Nación Argentina — bicameral legislature consisting of the Cámara de Diputados (257 members) and the Senado (72 members). Citizens can contact representatives through diputados.gov.ar and senado.gob.ar. The Comisión de Energía y Combustibles handles energy legislation. Argentina's NDC — Argentina submitted an updated NDC in 2021 targeting emissões of no more than 349 MtCO₂e by 2030. Climate Action Tracker rates this as 'highly insufficient.'

AT.s: Wealthy EU economia ($534.8B PIB) with strong manufacturing (15.1% of PIB). Low direct fossil fuel exposure but significant UPF and financial sector PST. Major transit hub for Russian gas (Baumgarten hub). High healthcare expenditure (11.83% PIB) suggests diet-related disease burden.

sectors.w: Austria's healthcare system (ASVG social insurance) tracks diet-related disease costs with high granularity. Quando per-product-category healthcare costs are published alongside retail revenue, W se torna computável from bilateral UPF transactions.
bp: Implementar front-of-pack health-impact labelling through EU Farm to Fork Strategy. Quando consumers see welfare costs at point of sale, the interesse sobreposto between UPF manufacturers and retailers in high-margin processed products enfraquece.

Mandate publication of diet-attributable healthcare costs per product category using ASVG social insurance data. Austria's comprehensive health insurance system generates the data needed to make W computable.
Not fully breakable. Ultra-processing alters food matrix in ways that affect metabolic response. Reformarulation mandates can reduce but not eliminate dependência do sistema.
a: Report misleading health claims on food products to the Bundeswettbewerbsbehörde (Federal Competition Authority)
Document undisclosed processing methods — report to AGES (Austrian Agency for Health and Food Safety)
Use the HinweisgeberInnenschutzgesetz (Whistleblower Protection Act, 2023) for protected reporting
Reformarulate product lines to reduce ultra-processing — invest in minimally processed alternatives
Voluntarily adopt front-of-pack nutritional labelling ahead of EU mandate
Commission health-impact assessment of product portfolio using national health insurance data
BMSGPK: Implementar mandatory front-of-pack health labelling for UPF products
AGES: Publish annual diet-attributable disease burden data linked to specific product categories
Hauptverband der Sozialversicherungsträger: Release anonymized diet-related healthcare cost data
Contacte o seu member of the Nationalrat or Bundesrat para exigir mandatory health-impact labelling on ultra-processed foods
Support EU-wide adoption of front-of-pack nutritional labelling
Advocate for inclusion of diet-attributable health costs in national accounting
Pressionar por WHO-aligned UPF marketing restrictions through EU legislative process
i: Bundeswettbewerbsbehörde (BWB / Federal Competition Authority) — Austria's competition authority, investigating unfair commercial practices including misleading advertising. AGES (Agentur für Gesundheit und Ernährungssicherheit / Austrian Agency for Health and Food Safety) — the federal agency responsible for food safety, nutrition surveillance, and public health. AGES conducts food inspections and risk assessments. HinweisgeberInnenschutzgesetz (HSchG, 2023) — Austria's transposition of the EU Whistleblower Directive, providing protection for employees reporting violations of EU or national law.

EU Farm to Fork Strategy — includes proposals for mandatory front-of-pack nutrition labelling across the EU. Austrian food companies that move early gain competitive advantage in the EU single market. EFSA (European Food Safety Authority) — provides scientific opinions that shape EU food regulation applicable in Austria.

BMSGPK (Bundesministerium für Soziales, Gesundheit, Pflege und Konsumentenschutz / Federal Ministry of Social Affairs, Health, Care and Consumer Protection) — oversees food safety policy, nutrition guidelines, and consumer protection. Hauptverband der österreichischen Sozialversicherungsträger (now Dachverband) — the umbrella organization of Austrian social insurance institutions, managing healthcare data for the entire insured population.

Österreichisches Parlament — Austria's bicameral parliament consisting of the Nationalrat (183 members) and the Bundesrat (61 members). Citizens can contact representatives through parlament.gv.at. The Gesundheitsausschuss (Health Committee) handles health and food policy legislation.

AZ.s: Oil-dependent economia with 29.57% combined oil and gas rents ($22B). SOCAR (State Oil Company) dominates. Hosted COP29 (2024). Authoritarian governance with limited sociedade civil space. Caspian Sea oil and gas fields (ACG, Shah Deniz) are mature.

sectors.w: Azerbaijan's oil production is declining from mature Caspian fields (ACG peaked in 2010). As production costs rise and reserves deplete, the interesse sobreposto between SOCAR and international partners (BP) enfraquece — diversification becomes economically necessary.
bp: Accelerate post-oil diversification using SOFAZ (State Oil Fund) reserves. Azerbaijan's $50B+ sovereign wealth fund can finance energia renovável and non-oil economic development. Quando SOFAZ investment returns from renewables exceed declining oil returns, the interesse sobreposto breaks.
Implementar por campo emissões disclosure for all Caspian operations. BP already publishes emissões data for ACG — extending to all SOCAR operations makes W computable across the sector.
Não é possível quebrar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report ambiental violations at oil facilities to the Ministry of Ecology and Natural Resources
Document emissões underreporting through internal SOCAR channels
Report ambiental concerns to international partners (BP) through their compliance systems
SOCAR: Desenvolver binding energia renovável transition plan with interim emissões targets
BP Azerbaijan: Extend ACG emissões transparência standards to all partner operations
SOFAZ: Adotar ESG investment criteria excluding new fossil fuel development
Ministry of Ecology: Fortalecer ambiental standards for offshore Caspian operations
EITI Azerbaijan: Expand reporting to include ambiental impact data per production block
Tariff Council: Reformar energy pricing to remove implicit fossil fuel consumption subsidies
Leverage Azerbaijan's COP29 presidency legacy to strengthen national climate commitments
Advogar através de the Tehran Convention for Caspian Sea ambiental protection standards
Support EITI enhanced reporting with ambiental metrics
Pressionar por European Energy Charter Treaty enforcement on ambiental standards in transit countries
i: Ministry of Ecology and Natural Resources — Azerbaijan's autoridade ambiental, responsible for ambiental monitoring, permitting, and enforcement. The Ministry oversees ambiental impact assessments for energy projects. SOCAR (State Oil Company of the Azerbaijan Republic) — the national oil company controlling all upstream and downstream petroleum operations. Note: Azerbaijan has no comprehensive denunciante protection law. Civil society space is severely constrained; independent media and NGOs face significant operational barriers.

SOCAR — Azerbaijan's state oil company, operating the country's refining, petrochemical, and upstream assets. SOCAR also has international operations in Turkey, Georgia, and Europe. BP — the largest international operator in Azerbaijan, managing the Azeri-Chirag-Deepwater Gunashli (ACG) oilfield and Shah Deniz gas field through Production Sharing Agreements. SOFAZ (State Oil Fund of the Republic of Azerbaijan) — the sovereign wealth fund managing Azerbaijan's petroleum revenues, with assets exceeding $50B.

Azerbaijan EITI — Azerbaijan was an early EITI member (2003) and publishes annual reports on extractive industry revenues. The EITI process provides one of the few independent transparência mechanisms in the sector. Tariff (Price) Council — sets regulated energy prices in Azerbaijan. Tehran Convention — the Framework Convention for the Protection of the Marine Environment of the Caspian Sea, which governs ambiental standards for offshore operations.

Milli Majlis (National Assembly of Azerbaijan) — unicameral parliament with 125 members. Note: Azerbaijan is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House; elections are not considered free and fair by international observers. Civic engagement channels are limited. International pressure operates primarily through multilateral forums including the Council of Europe (Azerbaijan is a member), OSCE, and the EITI Board. The EU-Azerbaijan Partnership and Cooperation Agreement provides a framework for dialogue on energy and ambiental issues.

BA.s: Post-conflict economia ($29.6B PIB) with complex governance (two entities: Federation and Republika Srpska). Coal-dependent energy sector and manufacturing (12.7% PIB). candidato à UE country. Significant coal mineração and thermal power generation.

sectors.w: Bosnia's coal sector is heavily subsidized through below-market electricity pricing and state employment guarantees. EU accession requires alignment with the EU ETS — when carbon pricing applies, the interesse sobreposto between coal producers and the state employment system breaks.
bp: EU accession alignment with EU ETS carbon pricing. Quando Bosnian coal power must pay the carbon price (~€90/ton), coal-fired electricity becomes uncompetitive with imported renewables. The interesse sobreposto between mine operators and state employment programs breaks.

Mandatory emissões monitoring and disclosure per power plant. Energy Community Treaty membership requires EU-aligned ambiental reporting — implementing this makes W computable from electricity generation data.
Não é possível quebrar. Coal combustion releases CO₂, SO₂, and particulate matter by physical process.
a: Report ambiental violations at coal mines and power plants to entity-level ambiental inspectorates
Document health impacts in communities near coal power plants — report to public health institutes
Report corruption in coal procurement to the Agency for Prevention of Corruption and Coordination of the Fight Against Corruption
State electricity companies (EPBiH, ERS): Desenvolver coal phase-out timelines aligned with Energy Community Treaty obligations
Investir em energia renovável capacity to replace aging coal plants
Implementar emissões monitoring per facility ahead of EU ETS alignment
DERK (State Electricity Regulatory Commission): Reformar tariffs to reflect true ambiental costs of coal generation
Entity ambiental agencies: Aplicar air quality standards at coal power plants
Energy Community Secretariat: Use dispute resolution to enforce ambiental compliance
Contacte o seu member of the Parlamentarna skupština para exigir coal phase-out timelines aligned with Energy Community obligations
Support EU accession negotiations contingent on energy transition commitments
Advocate for just transition funding for coal-dependent communities through IPA III (EU Instrument for Pre-Accession)
Pressionar por Energy Community Treaty enforcement on air quality standards
i: Environmental inspection is split between the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina and Republika Srpska, each maintaining separate ambiental agencies. Federation: Federalni inspektorat — ambiental inspectorate. Republika Srpska: Republička uprava za inspekcijske poslove — inspection administration. Agency for Prevention of Corruption and Coordination of the Fight Against Corruption (APIK) — state-level anti-corruption body. Bosnia lacks comprehensive denunciante protection legislation, though draft laws have been discussed as part of EU accession reforms.

EPBiH (Elektroprivreda Bosne i Hercegovine) — the Federation's state electricity company, operating coal-fired power plants and hydropower. ERS (Elektroprivreda Republike Srpske) — Republika Srpska's state electricity company. Both companies operate aging coal infrastructure (many plants dating to Yugoslav era) and are major employers in mineração communities.

DERK (Državna regulatorna komisija za električnu energiju / State Electricity Regulatory Commission) — the state-level electricity regulator. FERK and RERS are entity-level regulators. Energy Community Treaty — Bosnia is a contracting party, committed to implementing EU energy and ambiental acquis including Large Combustion Plants Directive and Industrial Emissions Directive.

Parlamentarna skupština Bosne i Hercegovine (Parliamentary Assembly) — bicameral legislature consisting of the Predstavnički dom (House of Representatives, 42 members) and the Dom naroda (House of Peoples, 15 members). Bosnia's complex governance structure (Dayton Agreement) divides power between state, entity, and cantonal levels. Citizens can contact representatives through parlament.ba.

BD.s: Large economia ($450.1B PIB) driven by garment manufacturing (21.9% mfg share of PIB). High agricultural employment (11.2% PIB). Extreme climate vulnerability — low-lying delta at severe flood and cyclone risk. One of the world's most densely populated countries.

sectors.w: Bangladesh's rice monoculture depletes groundwater (arsenic contamination) and reduces soil biodiversity. Satellite-based crop monitoring and water quality data from BWDB can make W computable from agricultural output and water extraction volumes.
bp: Reformar agricultural subsidies from input-based (fertilizer, diesel) to outcome-based (soil health, water efficiency). Quando subsidies reward diversification rather than rice intensification, o interesse sobreposto em monoculture between farmers and input suppliers enfraquece.

Mandatory groundwater extraction monitoring and arsenic contamination mapping published at upazila level. BWDB already collects water data — extending to public disclosure linked to agricultural output makes W computable.

Parcialmente quebrável. Intensive rice cultivation in deltaic conditions inherently alters hydrology and nutrient cycling. However, System of Rice Intensification (SRI) and crop diversification can reduce dependência do sistema.
a: Report pesticide misuse or adulteration to DAE (Department of Agricultural Extension)
Document groundwater over-extraction or arsenic contamination — report to DPHE (Department of Public Health Engineering)
Report food safety violations in agricultural supply chains to BFSA (Bangladesh Food Safety Authority)
Agricultural input companies: Desenvolver integrated pest management products as alternatives to chemical-intensive packages
Investir em climate-resilient crop varieties through partnerships with BRRI (Bangladesh Rice Research Institute)
Adotar water-use efficiency targets across supply chains
Ministry of Agriculture: Reformar subsidy structure from input-based to outcome-based
BWDB: Implementar mandatory groundwater monitoring networks in intensive agricultural zones
DoE (Department of Environment): Aplicar regulations on agricultural chemical runoff into waterways
Contacte o seu member of the Jatiya Sangsad para exigir reform of agricultural subsidies toward climate resilience and crop diversification
Support Bangladesh's Climate Vulnerable Forum leadership in advocating for agricultural adaptation finance
Advocate for inclusion of groundwater depletion costs in national PIB accounting
Pressionar por international climate finance to support agricultural diversification away from rice monoculture
i: DAE (Department of Agricultural Extension) — the primary government agency for agricultural technical support and extension services, operating at union, upazila, and district levels. DPHE (Department of Public Health Engineering) — responsible for water supply and sanitation, including monitoring arsenic contamination in groundwater (affecting ~35 million people). BFSA (Bangladesh Food Safety Authority) — established in 2015 to regulate food safety across the supply chain. Bangladesh lacks comprehensive denunciante protection legislation.

BRRI (Bangladesh Rice Research Institute) — the national research institute developing improved rice varieties, including flood-tolerant (BRRI dhan52), salt-tolerant, and drought-resistant varieties. BARI (Bangladesh Agricultural Research Institute) — conducts research on non-rice crops including vegetables, pulses, and oilseeds. BADC (Bangladesh Agricultural Desenvolverment Corporation) — the government agency responsible for seed production and distribution.

Ministry of Agriculture — oversees agricultural policy, subsidies, and development programs. BWDB (Bangladesh Water Desenvolverment Board) — responsible for flood control, drainage, and irrigation infrastructure. The BWDB manages the country's water resource data system. DoE (Department of Environment) — Bangladesh's ambiental regulatory agency, responsible for ambiental clearance of projects and monitoring pollution.

Jatiya Sangsad (National Parliament of Bangladesh) — unicameral legislature with 350 members (300 directly elected, 50 reserved for women). Citizens can contact representatives through parliament.gov.bd. The Standing Committee on Ministry of Agriculture handles agricultural policy. Bangladesh Climate Change Strategy and Action Plan (BCCSAP) — the national framework for climate adaptation and mitigation, including agricultural resilience.

BE.s: EU and NATO headquarters economia ($671.4B PIB). Services-dominant (71% PIB). Major port (Antwerp — Europe's second-largest) handles significant fossil fuel and petrochemical throughput. High healthcare expenditure (10.8% PIB). Federal state with three regions.

sectors.w: Belgium's comprehensive INAMI/RIZIV health insurance system tracks healthcare costs with high granularity. Publishing diet-attributable disease costs per product category makes W computable from retail sales data.
bp: Implementar front-of-pack health labelling (Belgium adopted Nutri-Score voluntarily in 2019). Extending to mandatory application and adding health-cost information enfraquece o interesse sobreposto em high-margin UPF.
INAMI/RIZIV publication of anonymized diet-attributable healthcare cost data per product category. Belgium's universal health insurance generates comprehensive data to make W computable.
Not fully breakable. Ultra-processing alters food matrix affecting metabolic response. Reformarulation mandates reduce but do not eliminate dependência do sistema.
a: Report misleading health claims to SPF Économie (Federal Public Service Economy)
Document food safety violations — report to AFSCA/FAVV (Federal Agency for the Safety of the Food Chain)
Use the Belgian denunciante protection law (transposition of EU Directive 2019/1937) for protected reporting
Reformarulate product lines to reduce ultra-processing
Extend voluntary Nutri-Score adoption to all product lines
Commission health-impact assessment of product portfolio
SPF Santé publique: Make Nutri-Score mandatory for all packaged food products
AFSCA/FAVV: Fortalecer enforcement of marketing claims on UPF products
INAMI/RIZIV: Publish annual reports linking food consumption patterns to healthcare expenditure
Contacte o seu member of the Chambre des représentants / Kamer van Volksvertegenwoordigers para exigir mandatory health-impact labelling
Support EU-wide mandatory Nutri-Score through the Farm to Fork Strategy
Advocate for WHO-aligned UPF marketing restrictions
Pressionar por inclusion of diet-related health costs in national accounting frameworks
i: AFSCA/FAVV (Agence fédérale pour la sécurité de la chaîne alimentaire / Federaal Agentschap voor de Veiligheid van de Voedselketen) — Belgium's federal food safety agency, responsible for food chain safety from farm to fork. Conducts inspections, sampling, and enforcement. SPF Économie (Service public fédéral Économie) — the federal economic affairs service handling consumer protection and fair commercial practices.

Fevia (Federation of the Belgian Food Industry) — the trade association representing over 700 food companies in Belgium. Fevia's sustainability commitments influence industry practices. Belgium hosts major food companies including Puratos, Vandemoortele, and Lotus Bakeries.

SPF Santé publique (Federal Public Service Health, Food Chain Safety and Environment) — the federal ministry overseeing public health policy, food safety, and environment. INAMI/RIZIV (Institut national d'assurance maladie-invalidité / Rijksinstituut voor ziekte- en invaliditeitsverzekering) — Belgium's national health insurance institute, managing the compulsory health insurance system and healthcare cost data.

Parlement fédéral / Federaal Parlement — Belgium's bicameral parliament consisting of the Chambre des représentants / Kamer van Volksvertegenwoordigers (150 members) and the Sénat / Senaat (60 members). Citizens can contact representatives through lachambre.be / dekamer.be. Belgium's federal structure means health policy is shared between federal and regional (community) governments.

BF.s: Low-income Sahelian economia ($23.1B PIB) with dominant gold mineração sector (min=15.45% PIB). Agriculture employs 80%+ of population (20.9% PIB). Military junta since 2022 coup. Severe security challenges (jihadist insurgency).

sectors.w: Burkina Faso's gold mineração generates cyanide contamination, mercury pollution (artisanal mineração), and community displacement. Quando ambiental and health costs per mine are published alongside production revenue, W se torna computável.
bp: Mandate ambiental bonds and community benefit agreements for all industrial mineração licenses. Quando mineração companies must pre-fund ambiental remediation and community compensation, o interesse sobreposto em extraction without accountability enfraquece.

Publish por mina ambiental impact data (water quality, air quality, land disturbance) alongside production revenue through EITI reporting. Burkina Faso's EITI membership provides the framework — extending to ambiental data makes W visible.

Parcialmente quebrável. Open-pit gold mineração inherently disturbs landscapes and generates toxic waste. However, improved tailings management and mercury-free artisanal methods can reduce dependência do sistema.
a: Report ambiental violations at mine sites to BUNEE (Bureau National des Évaluations Environnementales)
Document community health impacts near mineração operations through local health clinics
Report illegal artisanal mineração activities and mercury use to provincial authorities
Mining companies (Endeavour Mining, IAMGOLD, Nordgold): Implementar Global Industry Standard on Tailings Management
Adotar EITI enhanced reporting including ambiental impact metrics
Investir em mercury-free processing technologies for artisanal mineração support programs
Ministry of Mines: Fortalecer ambiental conditions in mineração permits and enforce compliance
BUMIGEB (Bureau des Mines et de la Géologie): Improve artisanal mineração regulation to reduce mercury use
EITI-BF: Expand reporting to include ambiental and community impact data
Advogar através de ECOWAS for regional mineração ambiental standards
Support African Mining Vision implementation for sustainable resource governance
Pressionar por international mineração companies to adopt binding ambiental standards through OECD Due Diligence Guidance
Support Kimberley Process-style certification for gold supply chains to reduce conflict mineral financing
i: BUNEE (Bureau National des Évaluations Environnementales) — Burkina Faso's national ambiental assessment authority, responsible for ambiental impact assessments of mineração projects. Ministère de l'Environnement, de l'Énergie, de l'Eau et de l'Assainissement — oversees ambiental policy and regulation. Note: Under military rule since 2022, institutional capacity is constrained and sociedade civil space is restricted.

Major mineração companies operating in Burkina Faso include Endeavour Mining (Houndé, Wahgnion mines), IAMGOLD (Essakane mine), and Nordgold (Bissa-Bouly). These international companies are subject to home-country ESG reporting requirements and international mineração standards (ICMM, IFC Performance Standards).

Ministère des Mines et des Carrières — the ministry responsible for mineração policy, licensing, and regulation. BUMIGEB (Bureau des Mines et de la Géologie du Burkina) — the geological survey and mineração technical agency. EITI Burkina Faso — Burkina Faso has been an EITI member since 2009, publishing reports on mineração revenues. The 2015 Mining Code establishes ambiental obligations for mineração companies.

Note: Burkina Faso is under military rule (Capitaine Ibrahim Traoré since 2022). The Assemblée Législative de Transition (Transitional Legislative Assembly) replaced the elected National Assembly. Democratic institutions are suspended. International engagement channels: ECOWAS (Economic Community of West African States — though Burkina Faso announced withdrawal in 2024), African Union, and international development partners. The African Mining Vision (2009) provides a continental framework for sustainable mineração governance.

BG.s: membro da UE economia ($113.3B PIB) with legacy coal dependence (Maritsa East complex is one of Europe's largest lignite basins). Services-dominant (63.8% PIB). EU accession has driven institutional modernization but coal transition remains politically sensitive.

sectors.w: Bulgaria's coal sector is sustained by implicit subsidies (below-market electricity pricing, state employment). EU ETS carbon pricing makes lignite uncompetitive — when full carbon costs are internalized, the interesse sobreposto between mine operators and state employment programs breaks.
bp: Full alignment with EU ETS free allowance phase-out. Quando Bulgarian lignite plants pay the EU carbon price without free allowances, coal-fired electricity becomes uncompetitive. The interesse sobreposto breaks when renewable alternatives are cheaper.

Mandatory por usina emissões and health-impact disclosure. Bulgaria's lignite plants (Maritsa East I, II, III) are among Europe's worst air polluters. Publishing health costs per MWh makes W computable from electricity generation data.
Não é possível quebrar. Lignite combustion releases CO₂, SO₂, NOx, and particulate matter by physical process.
a: Report emissões violations at coal plants to RIOSV (Regional Inspectorates of Environment and Water)
Document health impacts in communities near Maritsa East complex — report to RZI (Regional Health Inspectorates)
Use Bulgaria's denunciante protection law (transposition of EU Directive 2019/1937) for protected reporting
Maritsa East complex operators: Desenvolver coal phase-out plans with worker transition programs
Investir em energia renovável capacity (Bulgaria has strong solar potential — 1,500+ kWh/m²/year)
Adotar per-unit emissões reporting aligned with EU taxonomy
KEVR (Energy and Water Regulatory Commission): Reformar electricity tariffs to reflect carbon costs
Ministry of Energy: Desenvolver binding coal phase-out timeline aligned with EU Green Deal
Ministry of Environment: Aplicar Industrial Emissions Directive compliance at all coal plants
Contacte o seu member of the Narodno Sabranie (National Assembly) para exigir a binding coal phase-out timeline with just transition funding
Support EU Just Transition Fund allocation for Stara Zagora and other coal-dependent regions
Advocate for accelerated EU ETS free allowance phase-out
Pressionar por Territorial Just Transition Plans that include concrete retraining and economic diversification measures
i: RIOSV (Регионални инспекции по околната среда и водите / Regional Inspectorates of Environment and Water) — 16 regional ambiental agencies conducting inspections and enforcement. EEA (Изпълнителна агенция по околна среда / Executive Environment Agency) — the national ambiental monitoring agency collecting emissões and air quality data. Bulgaria transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive in 2023.

Maritsa East complex — comprises three lignite-fired power plants (Maritsa East I, II, III) and the associated Maritsa-Iztok mines. AES Bulgaria operates Maritsa East I; ContourGlobal operates Maritsa East III. The complex is the largest industrial employer in the Stara Zagora region. Mini Maritsa-Iztok — the estatal lignite mineração company supplying the Maritsa East plants.

KEVR (Комисия за енергийно и водно регулиране / Energy and Water Regulatory Commission) — Bulgaria's independent regulador de energia, setting electricity tariffs and licensing. Ministry of Energy — oversees energy policy including the National Energy and Climate Plan (NECP). Bulgaria's NECP has been criticized for insufficient coal phase-out ambition.

Narodno Sabranie (Народно събрание / National Assembly of Bulgaria) — unicameral parliament with 240 members. Citizens can contact representatives through parliament.bg. The Комисия по енергетика (Energy Committee) handles energy legislation. EU Just Transition Fund — Bulgaria is eligible for significant JTF funding to support coal transition in affected regions. Bulgaria's Territorial Just Transition Plans cover Stara Zagora, Kyustendil, and Pernik regions.

BH.s: Small island petrostate ($47.1B PIB) with 16.64% combined oil and gas rents. Bahrain's Abu Safa offshore field (shared with Saudi Arabia) provides the majority of oil revenue. Diversifying into financial services (Bahrain Financial Harbour) and aluminum (Alba smelter).

sectors.w: Bahrain's oil reserves are the smallest among GCC states (declining production from mature fields). The economic case for diversification is strongest here — when post-oil planning makes renewable investment more attractive than marginal barrel extraction, the interesse sobreposto breaks.
bp: Accelerate Economic Vision 2030 diversification away from oil dependence. Bahrain's small size and advanced financial sector make it the most transition-ready GCC economia. Quando financial services and technology revenue exceeds oil revenue, o interesse sobreposto em extraction breaks.
Publish por campo ambiental and emissões data for all Bahrain Petroleum Company (BAPCO) operations. Making lifecycle emissões visible per barrel makes W computable from production data.
Não é possível quebrar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report ambiental violations at BAPCO facilities to the Supreme Council for Environment
Document emissões or safety concerns through internal BAPCO compliance channels
Report concerns to Bahrain's National Audit Office regarding mismanagement of petroleum revenues
BAPCO: Accelerate the BAPCO Modernization Program to include energia renovável components
Mumtalakat (sovereign wealth fund): Increase energia renovável allocation in investment portfolio
Implementar TCFD-aligned climate disclosure for all operations
NOGA: Require emissões disclosure for all petroleum operations
SCE: Fortalecer ambiental standards for refinery operations
CBB (Central Bank of Bahrain): Integrate climate risk disclosure into financial sector regulation
Advogar através de GCC Secretariat for regional carbon pricing mechanisms
Support IRENA recommendations on GCC energy transition pathways
Pressionar por enhanced climate disclosure standards through the Bahrain Bourse ESG framework
Leverage Bahrain's UNFCCC commitments to strengthen national emissões reduction targets
i: Supreme Council for Environment (SCE) — Bahrain's autoridade ambiental, responsible for ambiental policy, permitting, and enforcement. SCE oversees ambiental impact assessments for industrial projects. BAPCO (Bahrain Petroleum Company) — the estatal oil company operating Bahrain's refinery and upstream assets. National Audit Office — conducts financial audits of government entities including BAPCO. Note: Bahrain has limited denunciante protection; sociedade civil space is constrained.

BAPCO (Bahrain Petroleum Company) — operates the Sitra refinery (267,000 bpd after modernization) and manages upstream production. Mumtalakat — Bahrain's sovereign wealth fund, managing state assets including BAPCO and Alba (Aluminium Bahrain). Nogaholding — the investment arm of Bahrain's National Oil and Gas Authority, overseeing the country's energy sector investments.

NOGA (National Oil and Gas Authority) — the government body responsible for oil and gas policy, licensing, and upstream regulation. CBB (Central Bank of Bahrain) — the financial sector regulator, which has begun integrating ESG considerations into banking supervision. Bahrain Economic Desenvolverment Board — coordinates the Economic Vision 2030 diversification strategy.

Council of Representatives (Majlis al-Nuwab) — the elected lower house of Bahrain's parliament (40 members) and the Shura Council (Majlis al-Shura, 40 appointed members). Note: Bahrain is a monarquia constitucional with significant royal authority. Political opposition is restricted. The Council of Representatives has limited legislative power. International engagement on climate issues operates through GCC coordination and UNFCCC processes.

BJ.s: Small West African economia ($21.5B PIB) with dominant agriculture sector (24.2% PIB, primarily cotton). Services growing (48.9% PIB). Cotton is the main export crop — Benin is Africa's largest cotton producer. Climate vulnerability through Sahel desertification and coastal erosion.

sectors.w: Benin's cotton monoculture degrades soil fertility and increases pesticide exposure. Quando soil health data and pesticide-related healthcare costs are published alongside cotton production revenue, W se torna computável from agricultural output data.
bp: Reformar cotton subsidies to reward soil health and crop diversification. Quando input subsidies (seeds, fertilizer, pesticides) are redirected from cotton intensification to mixed farming systems, the interesse sobreposto between cotton traders and input suppliers enfraquece.

Mandatory publication of soil health metrics (organic matter, nutrient balance) and pesticide-related health costs per cotton-producing commune. Making ambiental and health costs visible makes W computable.

Parcialmente quebrável. Cotton monoculture inherently depletes soil nutrients and requires pesticide application. However, integrated pest management and organic cotton production can reduce dependência do sistema — Benin has a growing organic cotton sector.
a: Report pesticide misuse or adulteration to DPQC (Direction de la Promotion de la Qualité et du Conditionnement des Produits Agricoles)
Document health impacts of pesticide exposure in cotton-growing communities — report to local health centers
Report corruption in cotton sector management to the Front des Organisations Nationales contre la Corruption (FONAC)
AIC (Association Interprofessionnelle du Coton): Promote integrated pest management to reduce chemical dependency
Investir em organic cotton certification and premium markets
Adotar soil health monitoring across cotton supply chains
MAEP (Ministry of Agriculture): Reformar cotton input subsidy programs to incentivize sustainable practices
ABE: Fortalecer ambiental impact monitoring in cotton-growing zones
INSAE (National Statistics Institute): Include soil health and agricultural ambiental indicators in national surveys
Contacte o seu Député in the Assemblée Nationale para exigir reform of cotton input subsidies toward sustainable agriculture
Advogar através de ECOWAS for regional agricultural sustainability standards
Pressionar por Cotton-4 (Benin, Burkina Faso, Chad, Mali) coordination on sustainable cotton production
Support WTO negotiations on cotton subsidy reform to level the playing field for African producers
i: DPQC (Direction de la Promotion de la Qualité et du Conditionnement des Produits Agricoles) — the government directorate responsible for agricultural product quality and standards. ABE (Agence Béninoise pour l'Environnement) — Benin's ambiental agency, responsible for ambiental impact assessment and monitoring. FONAC (Front des Organisations Nationales contre la Corruption) — a national anti-corruption coalition of sociedade civil organizations.

AIC (Association Interprofessionnelle du Coton) — the inter-professional cotton association coordinating relations between producers, ginners, and input suppliers. SODECO (Société de Développement du Coton) — the main cotton ginning company. The cotton sector was partially liberalized but remains state-coordinated through the AIC framework.

MAEP (Ministère de l'Agriculture, de l'Élevage et de la Pêche) — Benin's agriculture ministry, responsible for agricultural policy and cotton sector coordination. ABE (Agence Béninoise pour l'Environnement) — conducts ambiental monitoring and impact assessments. INSAE (Institut National de la Statistique et de l'Analyse Économique) — Benin's national statistics institute.

Assemblée Nationale du Bénin — unicameral parliament with 109 members. Citizens can contact their deputies through the official Assembly website. Note: Benin's democratic space has narrowed under recent constitutional reforms, though elections continue. Cotton-4 group — Benin, Burkina Faso, Chad, and Mali coordinate on cotton trade policy at the WTO, advocating against subsidies in developed countries that depress global cotton prices.

BN.s: Small wealthy petrostate ($15.3B PIB) with 24.23% combined oil and gas rents. Brunei Shell Petroleum (BSP, 50/50 JV with Shell) dominates the economia. Absolute monarchy. Wawasan Brunei 2035 diversification strategy underway but oil/gas remain 60%+ of government revenue.

sectors.w: Brunei's oil production is in long-term decline (from 220,000 bpd peak to ~100,000 bpd). As reserves deplete, the interesse sobreposto between BSP and the government in continued extraction enfraquece — Wawasan 2035 diversification becomes economically necessary.
bp: Accelerate Wawasan 2035 economic diversification using Brunei Investment Agency (BIA) sovereign wealth. Quando non-oil sectors generate more government revenue than declining oil production, o interesse sobreposto em extraction breaks.
Implementar por campo emissões disclosure for all BSP and Total operations. Shell's global ESG reporting standards can be extended to Brunei operations to make W computable.
Não é possível quebrar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report ambiental violations at BSP facilities through Shell's global ethics and compliance channels
Document emissões data gaps — report to the Department of Environment, Parks and Recreation
Use BSP's internal reporting mechanisms for safety and ambiental concerns
BSP/Shell: Extend Shell's global net-zero commitments to Brunei operations
BIA (Brunei Investment Agency): Diversify sovereign wealth into energia renovável and technology
Implementar TCFD-aligned climate disclosure for all Brunei operations
PetroleumBRUNEI: Require emissões monitoring and disclosure for all production licenses
Department of Environment: Fortalecer ambiental impact assessment requirements
AMBD (Autoriti Monetari Brunei Darussalam): Integrate climate risk into financial supervision
Advogar através de ASEAN for regional energia renovável integration and emissões reduction targets
Support UNFCCC mechanisms for small petrostate transition planning
Pressionar por APEC energy working group initiatives on fossil fuel subsidy reform
Leverage Brunei's ASEAN chairmanship rotations to advance regional climate commitments
i: BSP (Brunei Shell Petroleum) — a 50/50 joint venture between the government of Brunei and Shell, the country's largest oil and gas producer. BSP operates onshore and offshore fields. Department of Environment, Parks and Recreation — under the Ministry of Desenvolverment, responsible for ambiental policy and regulation. Note: Brunei is an absolute monarchy with no elected legislature. There is no denunciante protection legislation. Reporting channels are primarily internal corporate mechanisms.

BSP (Brunei Shell Petroleum) — managed jointly by the Brunei government and Shell. BIA (Brunei Investment Agency) — the sovereign wealth fund managing Brunei's petroleum revenues (estimated $30-40B). Total E&P Borneo — operates deep water gas fields offshore Brunei. PetroleumBRUNEI — the national petroleum authority overseeing the sector.

PetroleumBRUNEI — the national petroleum authority responsible for policy, regulation, and management of Brunei's oil and gas resources. AMBD (Autoriti Monetari Brunei Darussalam) — Brunei's central bank and financial regulator. Department of Energy — under the Prime Minister's Office, coordinates energy policy and the Wawasan 2035 energy diversification strategy.

Note: Brunei is an absolute monarchy (Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah). There is no elected legislature — the Legislative Council (Majlis Mesyuarat Negara) is appointed and has advisory functions only. Civic engagement operates through petition to the Sultan and through ASEAN and international multilateral channels. ASEAN — Brunei is a founding member. ASEAN's Framework for Circular Economy and declaration on mudança climática provide regional engagement mechanisms.

BO.s: Resource-dependent economia ($54.9B PIB) with significant natural gas reserves (gas=1.85% rents) and mineração (min=5.91%, lithium in Salar de Uyuni — world's largest deposit). Agricultura a 8.8% PIB with soy expansion driving desmatamento. Gas production declining from mature fields.

sectors.w: Bolivia holds the world's largest lithium reserves in the Salar de Uyuni. Lithium extraction uses massive water volumes in arid regions. Quando water consumption and brine chemistry data are published alongside production revenue, W se torna computável.
bp: Require community benefit agreements and water rights guarantees for all lithium extraction. Quando indigenous communities (who hold constitutional land rights under Bolivia's 2009 Constitution) can negotiate fair terms, o interesse sobreposto em extraction without consent enfraquece.

Mandatory publication of water extraction volumes, brine chemistry changes, and downstream ecosystem data per lithium operation. Making ambiental costs visible makes W computable from production data.

Parcialmente quebrável. Lithium brine extraction inherently alters aquifer chemistry and reduces water availability in arid ecosystems. However, direct lithium extraction (DLE) technology can significantly reduce water consumption compared to evaporation ponds.
a: Report ambiental violations at mineração operations to the Autoridad de Fiscalización y Control Social de Bosques y Tierra (ABT)
Document water contamination or indigenous rights violations — report to the Defensoría del Pueblo
Report safety violations at mineração sites to the Ministry of Labor
YLB (Yacimientos de Litio Bolivianos): Adotar DLE technology to minimize water consumption
Implementar community benefit sharing agreements with affected indigenous communities
Adotar international sustainability standards (IRMA — Initiative for Responsible Mining Assurance)
Ministry of Mining: Fortalecer ambiental requirements for lithium extraction permits
MMAyA (Ministry of Environment and Water): Aplicar water extraction limits in Salar de Uyuni basin
AJAM: Require ambiental bonds for all mineração concessions
Contacte o seu Diputado or Senador in the Asamblea Legislativa Plurinacional para exigir mandatory prior consultation with indigenous communities for all lithium projects
Support ILO Convention 169 enforcement on indigenous consultation rights in extractive industries
Advocate for international standards on sustainable lithium extraction through the Lithium Triangle coordination (Bolivia, Argentina, Chile)
Pressionar por inclusion of water depletion costs in mineração revenue accounting
i: AJAM (Autoridad Jurisdiccional Administrativa Minera) — the mineração administrative authority responsible for granting and overseeing mineração concessions. Defensoría del Pueblo — Bolivia's human rights ombudsman, responsible for investigating complaints about government actions affecting fundamental rights, including indigenous and ambiental rights. Bolivia's 2009 Constitution (Article 30) — guarantees indigenous peoples' right to prior consultation on resource extraction in their territories.

YLB (Yacimientos de Litio Bolivianos) — Bolivia's state lithium company, managing lithium industrialization in the Salar de Uyuni. COMIBOL (Corporación Minera de Bolivia) — the state mineração corporation overseeing Bolivia's mineração sector. YPFB (Yacimientos Petrolíferos Fiscales Bolivianos) — the state hydrocarbons company.

Ministerio de Minería y Metalurgia — Bolivia's mineração ministry, responsible for mineração policy and regulation. MMAyA (Ministerio de Medio Ambiente y Agua) — the environment and water ministry, overseeing ambiental policy and water resource management. SENARECOM (Servicio Nacional de Registro y Control de la Comercialización de Minerales y Metales) — the national service for mineral commerce registration and control.

Asamblea Legislativa Plurinacional (Plurinational Legislative Assembly) — Bolivia's bicameral parliament consisting of the Cámara de Diputados (130 members) and the Cámara de Senadores (36 members). Bolivia's 2009 Constitution establishes a 'plurinational' state recognizing indigenous rights and communitarian democracia. ILO Convention 169 — Bolivia has ratified ILO Convention 169 on Indigenous and Tribal Peoples, requiring free, prior, and informed consultation on resource extraction in indigenous territories.

BS.s: Small island economia ($15.8B PIB) dominated by tourism and financial services (77.2% services). Extreme climate vulnerability — low-lying islands face existential risk from sea level rise and intensifying hurricanes (Hurricane Dorian, 2019, caused $3.4B damage — 25% of PIB).

sectors.w: The Bahamas' offshore financial center enables capital flows that may avoid tax obligations elsewhere. Quando beneficial ownership information and tax transparência data are published, the system welfare impact of offshore finance becomes computable.
bp: Implementar full beneficial ownership transparência in line with FATF recommendations. Quando the competitive advantage of Bahamian financial secrecy is eliminated through transparência, the interesse sobreposto between offshore service providers and tax-avoiding clients enfraquece.

Publish aggregate data on capital flows, tax rulings, and beneficial ownership through enhanced OECD Common Reporting Standard participation. Making tax revenue impacts visible to source jurisdictions makes W computable.

Parcialmente quebrável. Financial intermediation can relocate — but the Bahamas' geographic proximity to the US and established legal infrastructure create switching costs that give regulation leverage.
a: Report suspicious transactions to the Financial Intelligence Unit (FIU) of The Bahamas
Document beneficial ownership concealment — report to the Securities Commission of The Bahamas
Report regulatory violations to the Central Bank of The Bahamas
Adotar enhanced due diligence procedures for all client relationships
Implementar beneficial ownership transparência standards ahead of regulatory mandates
Integrate climate risk assessment into financial products and client advisory services
Central Bank: Fortalecer beneficial ownership disclosure requirements for all licensed financial institutions
Securities Commission: Aplicar FATF recommendations on transparência and anti-money laundering
Compliance Commission: Increase supervision of designated non-financial businesses and professions
Contacte o seu Member of Parliament in the House of Assembly para exigir enhanced financial transparência legislation
Support OECD Base Erosion and Profit Shifting (BEPS) Inclusive Framework participation
Leverage the Alliance of Small Island States (AOSIS) to link climate finance to financial transparência commitments
Advocate for Caribbean regional coordination on beneficial ownership transparência
i: FIU (Financial Intelligence Unit of The Bahamas) — the agency responsible for receiving, analyzing, and disseminating suspicious transaction reports. The FIU works with law enforcement to combat money laundering and terrorist financing. Securities Commission of The Bahamas — regulates the investment funds industry and securities market. Central Bank of The Bahamas — the prudential regulator of banks and trust companies.

The Bahamas is home to approximately 200 licensed banks and trust companies. Major international banks maintain operations in Nassau. The Bahamas Serviços Financeiros Board (BFSB) promotes the financial services sector and coordinates industry standards.

Central Bank of The Bahamas — the primary financial regulator and supervisor. The Compliance Commission — supervises non-bank financial service providers for anti-money laundering compliance. FATF (Financial Action Task Force) — The Bahamas has been subject to FATF enhanced monitoring and has implemented reforms to address identified deficiencies in its AML/CFT framework.

Parliament of The Bahamas — bicameral legislature consisting of the House of Assembly (39 members) and the Senate (16 appointed members). Citizens can contact their MP through the official government portal. The Bahamas is a Westminster-style parlamentar democracia. AOSIS (Alliance of Small Island States) — The Bahamas is an active member, advocating for climate action and sea level rise mitigation. CARICOM — provides regional coordination on financial regulation.

BW.s: Upper-middle-income economia ($19.4B PIB) with diamond mineração historically driving growth (Debswana JV with De Beers). Services-dominant (63.5% PIB). One of Africa's most stable democracies. Diversification challenge as lab-grown diamonds threaten natural diamond demand.

sectors.w: Lab-grown diamonds are disrupting the natural diamond market (30%+ of gem market by volume). As synthetic alternatives become indistinguishable and cheaper, the interesse sobreposto between Debswana and consumers in natural diamond premium enfraquece — Botswana must diversify.
bp: Accelerate economic diversification using Pula Fund sovereign wealth. Quando non-diamond revenue streams (tech, tourism, beef) exceed declining diamond revenues, o interesse sobreposto em continued diamond dependence breaks naturally.

Publish full lifecycle ambiental data (water use, land disturbance, energy consumption) per carat for all mineração operations. Making ambiental costs visible makes W computable from diamond production data.

Parcialmente quebrável. Open-pit diamond mineração inherently disturbs landscapes. However, Botswana's mines are in relatively low-biodiversity Kalahari terrain, and rehabilitation programs can reduce long-term dependência do sistema.
a: Report ambiental violations at mine sites to DEA (Department of Environmental Affairs)
Document community displacement or inadequate compensation — report to the Ombudsman
Report safety violations to the Department of Mines
Debswana: Desenvolver post-diamond economic transition plan with energia renovável investment
De Beers: Extend lifecycle ambiental reporting to all Botswana operations
Adotar Kimberley Process Certification Scheme enhanced standards including ambiental metrics
Ministry of Minerals and Energy: Require ambiental bonds for all mineração licenses
BURS (Botswana Unified Revenue Service): Ensure mineral royalties reflect true ambiental costs
Okavango Diamond Company: Lead on sustainability certification for Botswana diamonds
Contacte o seu Member of Parliament in the National Assembly para exigir a comprehensive post-diamond economic diversification strategy
Support African Union Mining Vision implementation for sustainable diamond governance
Advocate for Kimberley Process reform to include ambiental and social standards
Pressionar por SADC (Southern African Desenvolverment Community) coordination on mineração sustainability
i: DEA (Department of Environmental Affairs) — under the Ministry of Environment and Tourism, responsible for ambiental policy, impact assessment, and monitoring. Department of Mines — under the Ministry of Minerals and Energy, responsible for mineração safety regulation, licensing, and compliance monitoring. Office of the Ombudsman — investigates complaints against government agencies and public officials.

Debswana Diamond Company — a 50/50 joint venture between the Government of Botswana and De Beers Group, operating the Jwaneng and Orapa mines. Debswana is Botswana's largest private employer and the largest contributor to government revenue. De Beers Group (Anglo American subsidiary) — the global diamond company partnering with Botswana. The 2023 renegotiation of the Debswana agreement gave Botswana a greater share of rough diamond sales.

Ministry of Minerals and Energy — oversees mineração policy and the regulatory framework under the Mines and Minerals Act. BURS (Botswana Unified Revenue Service) — the tax authority responsible for collecting mineral royalties and taxes. Pula Fund — Botswana's sovereign wealth fund (managed by the Bank of Botswana), designed to save diamond revenues for future generations.

National Assembly of Botswana — unicameral parliament with 65 members (57 elected, 8 specially elected). Citizens can contact their MPs through the parlamentar website. Botswana is one of Africa's most stable multiparty democracies, with peaceful transfers of power since independence in 1966. The Ntlo ya Dikgosi (House of Chiefs) — an advisory body representing traditional leaders, with input on matters affecting tribal territories including mineração.

BY.s: State-controlled economia ($76B PIB) with significant manufacturing (20.3% PIB). Potash mineração (Belaruskali is one of the world's largest producers). Oil refining (Naftan, Mozyr) processes Russian crude. Authoritarian governance under Lukashenko since 1994.

sectors.w: Belarus is the world's third-largest potash producer (Belaruskali). Potash mineração generates massive salt waste (solukhards) that contaminate groundwater and rivers. Quando downstream water contamination data is published alongside potash production revenue, W se torna computável.
bp: International sanções (EU, US, UK since 2021) have already weakened the interesse sobreposto by restricting Belaruskali's market access. Quando sanctioned export revenues fall below ambiental remediation costs, the economic case for continued expansion breaks.

Satellite-based ambiental monitoring (salt waste accumulation, water contamination plumes) published by international organizations. Making ambiental damage visible from space bypasses domestic data suppression and makes W computable.
Parcialmente quebrável. Potash mineração inherently generates salt waste. However, solution mineração and backfill techniques can reduce surface waste accumulation and groundwater contamination.
a: Document ambiental violations through secure channels to international ambiental organizations
Report safety concerns through ILO complaint mechanisms
Provide data to international monitoring organizations (Belarusian diaspora networks)
International potash buyers: Demand ambiental certification as condition of purchase (post-sanções)
Apply IFC Performance Standards to any future investment in Belarusian mineração
Require independent ambiental audits as part of off-take agreements
International sanções bodies: Maintain and strengthen potash export sanções until ambiental and governance reforms are implemented
UNECE: Continue ambiental performance reviews of Belarus under the Convention on Environmental Impact Assessment (Espoo Convention)
IndustriALL: Monitor labor rights conditions at Belaruskali
Support maintenance of international sanções on Belaruskali until ambiental and governance reforms are implemented
Advogar através de UNECE for transboundary ambiental monitoring of potash mineração impacts
Support Belarusian democratic opposition's platform for ambiental and governance reform
Pressionar por international ambiental damage assessment of Soligorsk mineração district
i: Note: Belarus under Lukashenko is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. Independent media, sociedade civil, and trade unions face severe repression. There is no effective denunciante protection. After the 2020 protests, thousands were imprisoned and major independent organizations were forcibly liquidated. Safe reporting channels include the Belarusian diaspora network, international trade union federations (IndustriALL Global Union), and international ambiental organizations.

Belaruskali — Belarus's estatal potash company, one of the world's largest potash producers. Belaruskali operates six mines near Soligorsk. The company has been under EU, US, and UK sanções since 2021 following the forced diversion of Ryanair Flight 4978 and repression of democratic opposition. BPC (Belarusian Potash Company) — the former exclusive export trading arm of Belaruskali (sanctioned). Competitors include Nutrien (Canada), Mosaic (US), and K+S (Germany).

Note: Domestic regulatory institutions (Ministry of Natural Resources and Environmental Protection) are not independent under autoritário governance. International mechanisms include: UNECE (United Nations Economic Commission for Europe) — Belarus is party to the Espoo Convention and the Convention on the Protection and Use of Transboundary Watercourses. EU sanções framework — Council Regulation (EU) restricting trade with Belaruskali and associated entities.

Note: Belarus has no functioning democratic legislature. The National Assembly (Natsionalnoye Sobranie) is not freely elected. International engagement channels: EU Eastern Partnership framework, OSCE (Organization for Security and Co-operation in Europe), United Nations Human Rights Council Universal Periodic Review. The Belarusian democratic opposition in exile (led by Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya) maintains a platform including ambiental governance reform.

CD.s: Resource-rich but impoverished economia ($71B PIB). Mining dominates (28.81% of PIB) — world's largest cobalt producer (70%+ global supply), significant copper, coltan, and tin. Conflict minerals remain a governance challenge. Agricultural employment is 60%+ but only 9.6% of PIB.

sectors.w: DRC's cobalt is essential for EV batteries — downstream manufacturers (Tesla, Samsung SDI, CATL) face increasing ESG pressure. Quando supply chain traceability makes mine-level ambiental and labor conditions visible to end consumers, W se torna computável from cobalt prices.
bp: Mandatory supply chain due diligence (EU Battery Regulation, US Dodd-Frank Section 1502) that requires full traceability to mine site. Quando artisanal miners must meet labor and ambiental standards to access global markets, o interesse sobreposto em unregulated extraction enfraquece.

Blockchain or RMI-style traceability systems linking por mina ambiental and labor data to each cobalt lot. The EU Battery Regulation (effective 2027) will require carbon footprint declarations and due diligence — making W computable per unit of cobalt.

Parcialmente quebrável. Mining inherently disturbs landscapes and generates waste. However, formalized artisanal mineração with proper safety and ambiental management can reduce dependência do sistema compared to unregulated extraction.
a: Report ambiental or labor violations at mine sites to international monitoring organizations (IPIS, Global Witness)
Document child labor or unsafe working conditions — report through responsible sourcing audit channels (RMI, LBMA)
Report illegal mineral exports to CEEC (Centre d'Évaluation, d'Expertise et de Certification des substances minérales)
Glencore, CMOC (China Moly): Implementar full supply chain traceability to mine site for all DRC cobalt
Adotar IRMA (Initiative for Responsible Mining Assurance) certification for industrial operations
Investir em formalization programs for artisanal mineração communities
Ministry of Mines: Aplicar 2018 Mining Code ambiental provisions including community development obligations
CEEC: Fortalecer mineral certification to include ambiental and labor compliance data
Cellule Technique de Coordination et de Planification Minière (CTCPM): Improve artisanal mineração registration and oversight
Contacte o seu Député national or Sénateur in the Parlement para exigir enforcement of the 2018 Mining Code ambiental provisions
Support EU Battery Regulation implementation requiring full cobalt supply chain traceability
Advogar através de the African Union for continental responsible mineração standards
Pressionar por OECD Due Diligence Guidance enforcement for all minerals sourced from DRC
i: CEEC (Centre d'Évaluation, d'Expertise et de Certification des substances minérales précieuses et semi-précieuses) — the DRC government agency responsible for evaluating, certifying, and controlling mineral exports. SAEMAPE (Service d'Assistance et d'Encadrement du Small Scale Mining) — the agency responsible for regulating artisanal and small-scale mineração. IPIS (International Peace Information Service) — an independent research institute mapping mineração sites, armed group presence, and supply chain risks in the DRC. Global Witness — international NGO investigating the links between natural resource exploitation and conflict.

Glencore — operates Mutanda and Katanga Mining (KCC) copper-cobalt mines, among the world's largest. CMOC (China Molybdenum Co.) — operates Tenke Fungurume, one of the world's largest copper-cobalt mines. Entreprise Générale du Cobalt (EGC) — state-backed enterprise intended to monopolize artisanal cobalt purchasing (established 2019). RMI (Responsible Minerals Initiative) — industry-led supply chain due diligence program used by electronics and automotive manufacturers.
