Ministry of Mines — oversees mineração policy, licensing, and the 2018 Mining Code (Code Minier), which increased royalty rates and introduced strategic substance classification for cobalt. CTCPM (Cellule Technique de Coordination et de Planification Minière) — the technical coordination unit for mineração sector planning. ITSCI (ITRI Tin Supply Chain Initiative) — a traceability program for tin, tantalum, and tungsten (3T) minerals from the DRC.

Parlement de la République démocratique du Congo — bicameral legislature consisting of the Assemblée nationale (500 members) and the Sénat (108 members). The DRC is a semi-presidential republic but governance capacity is limited outside major urban centers. EU Battery Regulation (2023/1542) — effective 2027, requires carbon footprint declarations, recycled content minimums, and supply chain due diligence for batteries containing cobalt. OECD Due Diligence Guidance for Responsible Supply Chains of Minerals from Conflict-Affected and High-Risk Areas — the international standard for mineral supply chain responsibility.

CG.s: Oil-dependent economia ($15.7B PIB) with 34.74% combined oil and gas rents — one of Sub-Saharan Africa's highest. TotalEnergies and Eni are major operators. Governance challenges with long-ruling president (Sassou Nguesso since 1997). Significant tropical forest cover.

sectors.w: Congo-Brazzaville's oil production is in long-term decline from mature offshore fields. As extraction costs rise and reserves deplete, the interesse sobreposto between international operators and government revenue enfraquece — diversification becomes necessary.
bp: Redirect oil revenues to economic diversification (forestry, agriculture, services) through transparent budget allocation. Quando non-oil sectors generate more employment and revenue, o interesse sobreposto em continued extraction from depleting fields breaks.
Implementar EITI-standard per-block revenue and emissões disclosure. Congo joined EITI in 2004 — extending to ambiental data makes W computable per production unit.
Não é possível quebrar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report ambiental violations at oil facilities to the Ministry of Environment
Document revenue discrepancies — report to EITI Congo multi-stakeholder group
Report concerns to international operator compliance channels (TotalEnergies, Eni)
TotalEnergies, Eni: Implementar Paris-aligned transition plans for Congo operations
SNPC: Desenvolver economic diversification strategy using petroleum revenues
Adotar per-block emissões reporting standards
Ministry of Hydrocarbons: Fortalecer ambiental conditions in production sharing contracts
EITI Congo: Expand reporting to include ambiental impact data
Central Bank (BEAC): Integrate resource revenue volatility risk into macroprudential supervision
Advogar através de CEMAC for regional ambiental standards in oil and gas extraction
Support EITI enhanced reporting with ambiental metrics
Pressionar por Central African Forest Initiative (CAFI) enforcement on forest protection as alternative revenue source
Leverage UNFCCC mechanisms for fossil fuel transition support
i: Ministère de l'Environnement, du Développement Durable et du Bassin du Congo — the environment ministry. SNPC (Société Nationale des Pétroles du Congo) — the national oil company. EITI Congo — the Republic of Congo has been an EITI member since 2004. Note: Whistleblower protections are minimal under current governance.

TotalEnergies — the largest oil operator in Congo-Brazzaville, operating the Moho Nord deepwater project and other fields. Eni — operates significant offshore concessions. SNPC (Société Nationale des Pétroles du Congo) — the national oil company holding state equity in all production sharing contracts.

Ministère des Hydrocarbures — oversees oil and gas policy and licensing. BEAC (Banque des États de l'Afrique Centrale) — the central bank of the CEMAC monetary zone (shared with Cameroon, CAR, Chad, Equatorial Guinea, Gabon). EITI Congo — publishes annual reports on extractive industry revenues.

Note: Congo-Brazzaville under President Sassou Nguesso is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. The Assemblée nationale (151 members) and Sénat (72 members) do not function as independent democratic institutions. International engagement channels: CEMAC (Communauté Économique et Monétaire de l'Afrique Centrale), African Union, CAFI (Central African Forest Initiative) — a partnership between Central African countries and donors to protect tropical forests.

CI.s: Largest economia in francophone West Africa ($87.1B PIB). World's largest cocoa producer (~45% of global supply). Agriculture is 15.9% of PIB with cocoa, coffee, and palm oil as main cash crops. Growing oil production (oil=0.7%). Rapid economic growth (~7% annual average).

sectors.w: Côte d'Ivoire's cocoa monoculture has driven 80%+ of original forest loss. EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) will require desmatamento-free supply chains — when per-farm desmatamento data is linked to cocoa transactions, W se torna computável.
bp: EU Deforestation Regulation compliance forces traceability to farm level. Quando cocoa buyers must verify zero-desmatamento, the interesse sobreposto between traders and smallholders in clearing forest for new plantations breaks — agroforestry becomes the viable model.

Mandatory per-farm ambiental data (tree cover, soil carbon, water use) linked to cocoa lot traceability. The Cocoa and Forests Initiative already creates a framework — extending to mandatory data makes W computable from cocoa prices.

Parcialmente quebrável. Cocoa monoculture inherently reduces biodiversity compared to native forest. However, cocoa agroforestry systems can maintain significant tree cover and biodiversity — Côte d'Ivoire's National REDD+ Strategy includes agroforestry targets.
a: Report illegal desmatamento for cocoa expansion to SODEFOR (Société de Développement des Forêts) or OIPR (Office Ivoirien des Parcs et Réserves)
Document child labor in cocoa production — report to CNS (Comité National de Surveillance des actions de lutte contre le travail des enfants)
Report pesticide misuse — notify ANADER (Agence Nationale d'Appui au Développement Rural)
Cocoa traders (Cargill, Barry Callebaut, Olam): Implementar full farm-level traceability ahead of EUDR deadline
Investir em cocoa agroforestry systems that maintain tree cover while improving yields
Adotar living income reference price commitments for cocoa farmers
CCC (Conseil du Café-Cacao): Aplicar traceability requirements and link farmgate prices to ambiental compliance
MINEDD (Ministry of Environment): Fortalecer enforcement against desmatamento in classified forests
ANADER: Scale agroforestry extension programs to all cocoa-growing regions
Contacte o seu Député in the Assemblée nationale para exigir enforcement of the Forest Code and protection of classified forests
Support EU Deforestation Regulation implementation with adequate transition support for smallholder farmers
Advogar através de the Cocoa and Forests Initiative for binding desmatamento targets
Pressionar por living income benchmarks in international cocoa pricing through ICCO
i: SODEFOR (Société de Développement des Forêts) — the state forestry company responsible for managing classified forests, many of which have been illegally cleared for cocoa. OIPR (Office Ivoirien des Parcs et Réserves) — manages national parks and protected areas. CNS (Comité National de Surveillance) — the national committee monitoring child labor in cocoa, coordinated with the International Cocoa Initiative (ICI). ANADER — the national rural development support agency providing agricultural extension services.

Major cocoa traders operating in Côte d'Ivoire: Cargill, Barry Callebaut, Olam International, Sucden. These companies intermediate the majority of Ivorian cocoa exports. Cocoa and Forests Initiative (CFI) — a public-private partnership between cocoa-producing countries (Côte d'Ivoire, Ghana) and leading chocolate companies to end desmatamento in cocoa supply chains. The Conseil du Café-Cacao (CCC) — the Ivorian cocoa board that sets farmgate prices and regulates the sector.

CCC (Conseil du Café-Cacao) — the government body regulating Côte d'Ivoire's cocoa and coffee sectors, setting minimum farmgate prices, managing quality standards, and overseeing exports. MINEDD (Ministère de l'Environnement et du Développement Durable) — the environment ministry responsible for forest protection, climate policy, and ambiental regulation.

Assemblée nationale de Côte d'Ivoire — unicameral parliament with 255 members. Citizens can contact deputies through the official Assembly website. Sénat — the upper house (99 members, established 2020). ICCO (International Cocoa Organization) — the intergovernmental body coordinating global cocoa policy, headquartered in Abidjan. EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR, 2023/1115) — requires EU importers to verify that commodities including cocoa are not linked to desmatamento after December 2020.

CL.s: Upper-middle-income economia ($330.3B PIB) with massive mineração sector (16.23% of PIB). World's largest copper producer (~27% of global supply) and second-largest lithium producer. Strong institutions and democratic governance. Renewable energy leader in Latin America (solar in Atacama).

sectors.w: Chile's copper and lithium mineração depletes water resources in the Atacama Desert (one of Earth's driest regions). Quando por mina water consumption data is published alongside production revenue, W se torna computável from mineração output data.
bp: Implementar water pricing that reflects true scarcity in mineração regions. Quando miners pay the real cost of water extraction in hyper-arid zones, o interesse sobreposto em water-intensive extraction methods enfraquece — desalination and dry processing become economically preferred.

Mandatory por mina water balance, tailings chemistry, and emissões disclosure. Chile's SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente) already collects ambiental data — making it publicly accessible per mine makes W computable.
Parcialmente quebrável. Mining inherently disturbs landscapes. However, dry processing, desalination, and tailings reprocessing can significantly reduce water dependence and waste generation.
a: Report ambiental violations to SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente)
Document water rights violations — report to DGA (Dirección General de Aguas)
Use Chile's denunciante law (Ley 21.015) and ambiental complaint channels (denuncia ambiental) for protected reporting
CODELCO (state copper company): Transition to desalinated water for all operations
Adotar Science Based Targets for emissões reduction across mineração operations
Implementar ICMM Mining Principles including tailings management standards
SMA: Increase ambiental monitoring frequency and enforce water extraction limits at mineração operations
Sernageomin: Fortalecer tailings dam safety standards (Chile has 740+ tailings facilities)
Ministry of Mining: Implementar the National Lithium Strategy with binding ambiental standards
Contacte o seu Diputado or Senador in the Congreso Nacional para exigir mandatory water-neutral mineração operations through desalination requirements
Support the Escazú Agreement on ambiental access rights in Latin America (Chile has not ratified)
Advocate for OECD Due Diligence Guidance enforcement for mineração supply chains
Pressionar por international coordination on critical mineral sustainability standards through the Minerals Security Partnership
i: SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente) — Chile's ambiental enforcement agency, responsible for monitoring compliance with ambiental permits (RCA — Resolución de Calificación Ambiental). SMA can impose fines up to $8.2M and order facility closure. DGA (Dirección General de Aguas) — the national water authority managing water rights and monitoring extraction. SEA (Servicio de Evaluación Ambiental) — conducts ambiental impact assessments for mineração projects.

CODELCO (Corporación Nacional del Cobre) — Chile's estatal copper company and the world's largest copper producer. CODELCO operates Chuquicamata, El Teniente, and other major mines. SQM (Sociedad Química y Minera de Chile) — one of the world's largest lithium producers, operating in the Salar de Atacama. Albemarle — US company operating lithium extraction in the Salar de Atacama alongside SQM. BHP, Anglo American — major international copper mineração companies operating in Chile.

Sernageomin (Servicio Nacional de Geología y Minería) — the national geological and mineração service, responsible for mine safety, tailings dam oversight, and geological surveys. Chile has over 740 tailings facilities, the largest inventory in the world. Ministry of Mining — oversees mineração policy including the 2023 National Lithium Strategy, which established state-led lithium development through public-private partnerships.

Congreso Nacional de Chile — bicameral legislature consisting of the Cámara de Diputadas y Diputados (155 members) and the Senado (50 members). Citizens can contact representatives through camara.cl and senado.cl. Chile is an OECD member with strong democratic institutions. Escazú Agreement — the Regional Agreement on Access to Information, Public Participation and Justice in Environmental Matters in Latin America and the Caribbean. Chile signed but has not ratified.

CM.s: Central African economia ($53.3B PIB) with oil production (2.94% combined rents) and significant agriculture (18.5% PIB — cocoa, palm oil, timber). Forest cover declining due to logging and agricultural expansion. Governance challenges and Anglophone crisis.

sectors.w: Cameroon's oil production is declining from mature fields. As extraction costs rise, the interesse sobreposto between operators (Perenco, Addax) and government revenue enfraquece — diversification to sustainable forestry and agriculture becomes economically rational.
bp: Redirect declining oil revenues to sustainable forest management and agricultural diversification. Cameroon's forests are a carbon sink worth more standing than cleared. Quando REDD+ payments exceed marginal oil revenue, o interesse sobreposto em continued extraction breaks.
Implementar EITI-standard per-block ambiental disclosure. Cameroon's EITI membership provides the framework — extending to ambiental data makes W computable.
Não é possível quebrar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report ambiental violations to MINEPDED (Ministry of Environment, Nature Protection and Sustainable Desenvolverment)
Document illegal logging or land seizures — report to international forestry monitoring organizations (Global Forest Watch)
Report through EITI Cameroon multi-stakeholder channels
Perenco, Addax/Sinopec: Implementar ambiental monitoring and emissões reporting for all Cameroon operations
SNH: Desenvolver post-oil diversification strategy
Adotar TCFD-aligned climate disclosure
MINMIDT (Ministry of Mines): Fortalecer ambiental conditions in production licenses
MINEPDED: Aplicar ambiental impact assessment requirements
EITI Cameroon: Expand reporting to ambiental data
Contacte o seu Député in the Assemblée nationale para exigir transparent management of declining oil revenues
Support CAFI (Central African Forest Initiative) for forest conservation as alternative revenue
Advogar através de CEMAC for regional ambiental standards
Pressionar por EITI enhanced reporting
i: MINEPDED (Ministère de l'Environnement, de la Protection de la Nature et du Développement Durable) — Cameroon's environment ministry. SNH (Société Nationale des Hydrocarbures) — the national hydrocarbons company managing state interests in oil and gas. EITI Cameroon — member since 2007. Note: Whistleblower protections are limited.

Perenco — the largest oil producer in Cameroon. Addax Petroleum (Sinopec subsidiary) — operates offshore concessions. SNH manages the state's petroleum interests and holds equity in production sharing contracts.

MINMIDT (Ministère des Mines, de l'Industrie et du Développement Technologique) — oversees mineração and industrial policy. CONAC (Commission Nationale Anti-Corruption) — Cameroon's anti-corruption commission.

Assemblée nationale du Cameroun — 180 members. Sénat — 100 members. Note: Cameroon's democratic institutions are constrained under long-ruling President Paul Biya (in power since 1982). International channels: CEMAC, African Union, CAFI.

CO.s: Major Latin American economia ($418.8B PIB) with significant oil (3.42% rents) and coal (0.73% rents) production. Ecopetrol is the largest company. Biodiversity hotspot (most biodiverse country per square kilometer). 2016 peace agreement with FARC opened new governance challenges in former conflict zones.

sectors.w: Colombia's current administration (Petro, 2022-) has announced a policy of no new oil exploration licenses. If maintained, o interesse sobreposto em fossil fuel expansion breaks as existing fields deplete. Colombia's renewable potential (hydro, solar, wind) provides viable alternatives.
bp: Maintain and implement the no-new-exploration-licenses policy while investing transition revenues in energia renovável. Quando energy investment flows shift from fossil to renewable, o interesse sobreposto em continued exploration breaks.

Mandatory por campo emissões disclosure for all Ecopetrol and international operator fields. Colombia's ANH (Agencia Nacional de Hidrocarburos) already collects production data — extending to emissões makes W computable.
Não é possível quebrar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report ambiental violations to ANLA (Autoridad Nacional de Licencias Ambientales)
Document community health impacts near oil operations — report to Defensoría del Pueblo
Report through Ecopetrol's ethics line or international operator compliance channels
Ecopetrol: Accelerate energy transition strategy with binding renewable targets
Implementar TCFD-aligned reporting across all operations
Investir em energia renovável in post-conflict zones to provide economic alternatives
MinAmbiente: Fortalecer ambiental licensing for extractive projects in biodiverse regions
CREG: Reformar energy tariffs to accelerate energia renovável deployment
ANH: Implementar por campo emissões monitoring
Contacte o seu Representante or Senador in the Congreso to support the energy transition framework legislation
Fortalecer Colombia's NDC with binding sectoral emissões reduction targets
Leverage OECD membership to advance ambiental governance standards
Support the Leticia Pact for Amazon conservation as alternative economic model
i: ANLA (Autoridad Nacional de Licencias Ambientales) — Colombia's ambiental licensing authority, responsible for ambiental impact assessment and compliance monitoring for extractive projects. Defensoría del Pueblo — the national human rights ombudsman. Contraloría General de la República — the national audit office overseeing public resource management including oil revenues.

Ecopetrol — Colombia's largest company (88.5% estatal), responsible for most domestic oil production. Ecopetrol has announced energy transition targets but continues significant upstream investment. ANH (Agencia Nacional de Hidrocarburos) — manages exploration and production contracts.

MinAmbiente (Ministerio de Ambiente y Desarrollo Sostenible) — Colombia's environment ministry. CREG (Comisión de Regulación de Energía y Gas) — the regulador de energiay commission. CAR (Corporaciones Autónomas Regionales) — regional ambiental authorities.

Congreso de la República de Colombia — bicameral legislature: Cámara de Representantes (188 members) and Senado (108 members). Contact through camara.gov.co and senado.gov.co. Colombia is an OECD member (2020) with strong democratic institutions. The 2016 Peace Agreement (Acuerdo de Paz) created new governance frameworks for former conflict zones that intersect with extractive industry regulation.

CR.s: Upper-middle-income economia ($95.4B PIB) with 98%+ renewable electricity generation. No military since 1948. Services-dominant (68.8% PIB). Leader in ecotourism and ambiental policy. Low direct fossil fuel extraction. Main PST exposure through agricultural monoculture (pineapple, banana, palm oil).

sectors.w: Costa Rica's pineapple and banana monoculture uses intensive pesticide application that contaminates waterways. Quando per-farm pesticide runoff and water contamination data is published alongside export revenue, W se torna computável.
bp: Reformar agricultural export incentives to reward biodiversity-positive farming. Quando export tax benefits require ambiental certification, the interesse sobreposto between monoculture producers and exporters in chemical-intensive production enfraquece.

Mandatory per-farm water quality monitoring downstream of monoculture operations. Costa Rica's AyA (water utility) and SINAC already collect water data — linking to agricultural output makes W computable.
Parcialmente quebrável. Tropical monoculture inherently reduces biodiversity. However, Costa Rica's Payment for Environmental Services (PSA) program demonstrates viable alternatives.
a: Report pesticide violations to SFE (Servicio Fitosanitario del Estado)
Document water contamination — report to MINAE (Ministry of Environment) or AyA
Use Costa Rica's labor protections and ambiental complaint mechanisms
Agricultural exporters (Del Monte, Dole, Chiquita): Adotar pesticide reduction targets
Investir em organic certification and agroforestry transitions
Implementar watershed-level ambiental monitoring
MAG (Ministry of Agriculture): Reformar export incentives to require ambiental certification
MINAE: Fortalecer pesticide regulations and enforce water quality standards near monoculture areas
SETENA: Require cumulative ambiental impact assessments for monoculture expansion
Contacte o seu Diputado in the Asamblea Legislativa para exigir pesticide reduction legislation for monoculture agriculture
Support the Escazú Agreement implementation (Costa Rica was an early advocate)
Leverage Costa Rica's ambiental reputation to advance international agricultural sustainability standards
Pressionar por green trade provisions in bilateral agreements
i: SFE (Servicio Fitosanitario del Estado) — the state phytosanitary service regulating pesticide use. MINAE (Ministerio de Ambiente y Energía) — the environment and energy ministry. AyA (Instituto Costarricense de Acueductos y Alcantarillados) — the national water utility. SETENA (Secretaría Técnica Nacional Ambiental) — conducts ambiental impact assessments.

Costa Rica hosts operations of major fruit multinationals: Del Monte, Dole, Chiquita. CORBANA (Corporación Bananera Nacional) — the national banana corporation coordinating the sector. CANAPEP (Cámara Nacional de Productores y Exportadores de Piña) — the pineapple producers' association.

MAG (Ministerio de Agricultura y Ganadería) — Costa Rica's agriculture ministry. The PSA (Pago por Servicios Ambientales) program — Costa Rica's pioneering payment for ambiental services program, which pays landowners to maintain forest cover, funded partly by a fuel tax.

Asamblea Legislativa de Costa Rica — unicameral parliament with 57 members. Citizens can contact representatives through asamblea.go.cr. Costa Rica is one of Latin America's strongest democracies and a global ambiental leader. The Escazú Agreement — Costa Rica was one of the first signatories of this regional agreement on ambiental access rights.

CU.s: Command economia ($107.4B PIB, though estimates vary due to dual currency system). Services-dominant (73.5% PIB, driven by healthcare, education, tourism). Minimal resource extraction. Economic crisis exacerbated by US embargo and collapse of Venezuelan oil subsidies.

sectors.w: Cuba's sugar industry collapsed after Soviet subsidies ended, but remaining agriculture (state farms, cooperatives) relies on monoculture patterns. Cuba's organic farming necessity (post-Soviet fertilizer shortage) demonstrates alternative models. Making soil health data visible breaks system independence.
bp: Expand the urban agriculture and organoponics model that Cuba developed out of necessity. Quando organic production meets domestic food needs more reliably than import-dependent monoculture, o interesse sobreposto em conventional agriculture breaks.
Publish soil health, water quality, and biodiversity data from Cuba's extensive agricultural research system. Cuba's scientific institutions (INIFAT, ICA) generate data that could make W computable.
Parcialmente quebrável. Cuba's experience with organic farming post-1991 shows that dependência do sistema on chemical inputs can be dramatically reduced through agroecological methods.
a: Document ambiental degradation through academic research channels
Report through workplace union structures (CTC — Central de Trabajadores de Cuba)
Engage with international agricultural research partnerships
State agricultural enterprises: Scale organoponics and agroecological methods
AZCUBA: Diversify sugar industry toward bioenergy and specialty products
International partners: Support technology transfer for sustainable agriculture
MINAG: Expand support for agroecological transition
CITMA: Fortalecer ambiental monitoring of agricultural practices
INIFAT: Publish research on agroecological transition outcomes
Support FAO technical cooperation programs for Cuban agricultural sustainability
Advogar através de CELAC for regional agricultural knowledge exchange
Pressionar por relaxation of US embargo restrictions on agricultural technology transfer
Support South-South cooperation on agroecological methods developed in Cuba
i: Note: Cuba is a estado de partido único. Independent sociedade civil, media, and labor organizations are not permitted. Reporting channels operate within state structures. CTC (Central de Trabajadores de Cuba) — the state-controlled trade union federation. INIFAT (Instituto Nacional de Investigaciones Fundamentales en Agricultura Tropical) — the national agricultural research institute. CITMA (Ministerio de Ciencia, Tecnología y Medio Ambiente) — the science and environment ministry.

AZCUBA (Empresa Azucarera) — Cuba's state sugar enterprise. State agricultural enterprises and UBPCs (Unidades Básicas de Producción Cooperativa) — cooperatives managing most agricultural production. Cuba's urban agriculture program is one of the world's most successful, producing significant food in cities through organoponics.

MINAG (Ministerio de la Agricultura) — Cuba's agriculture ministry. CITMA — oversees ambiental policy and scientific research. The Asociación Nacional de Agricultores Pequeños (ANAP) — the national small farmers' association, which has been a key partner in agroecological transition.

Note: Cuba's Asamblea Nacional del Poder Popular (605 members) is the legislature but functions within a one-party system. International engagement channels: FAO (Cuba has received recognition for reducing hunger), CELAC (Community of Latin American and Caribbean States), NAM (Non-Aligned Movement). Cuba's agroecological experience is studied globally as a model for post-petrochemical agriculture.

CY.s: Small EU island economia ($37.6B PIB) with dominant services sector (76.5% PIB, driven by financial services, shipping, and tourism). No significant resource extraction. Post-2013 banking crisis reforms strengthened financial regulation. Division between Republic of Cyprus and northern territory.

sectors.w: Cyprus's financial services sector has historically facilitated capital flows from Russia and other jurisdictions. EU AML directives and beneficial ownership transparência requirements make the welfare impact of opaque financial intermediation computable.
bp: Full implementation of EU Anti-Money Laundering Directives and beneficial ownership transparência. Quando financial secrecy is eliminated, the interesse sobreposto between Cypriot service providers and clients seeking opacity enfraquece.

Mandatory publication of Country-by-Country Reporting (CbCR) data for entities registered in Cyprus. EU ATAD and DAC6 frameworks provide the mechanism — making tax planning structures visible breaks system independence.
Parcialmente quebrável. Financial intermediation can relocate — but Cyprus's membro da UEship, common law heritage, and timezone advantages create switching costs.
a: Report suspicious transactions to MOKAS (Unit for Combating Money Laundering)
Document beneficial ownership concealment — report to CySEC (Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission)
Use EU Whistleblower Directive protections (transposed 2022)
Implementar enhanced due diligence for all high-risk client relationships
Adotar full beneficial ownership transparência ahead of EU AMLD6 requirements
Integrate ESG criteria into financial advisory services
CySEC: Fortalecer beneficial ownership verification requirements
CBC: Aplicar enhanced AML due diligence for correspondent banking relationships
ICPAC (Institute of Certified Public Accountants): Ensure audit firms enforce transparência standards
Contacte o seu Βουλευτής in the Βουλή των Αντιπροσώπων (House of Representatives) para exigir full implementation of EU AML directives
Support EU AMLD6 negotiations for enhanced transparência requirements
Advocate for OECD BEPS Inclusive Framework strengthening
Pressionar por EU-wide beneficial ownership registry interconnection
i: MOKAS (Μονάδα Καταπολέμησης Αδικημάτων Συγκάλυψης / Unit for Combating Money Laundering) — Cyprus's financial intelligence unit. CySEC (Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission) — the securities and financial markets regulator. CBC (Central Bank of Cyprus) — the prudential supervisor of banks. Cyprus transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive in 2022.

Cyprus hosts approximately 40 licensed banks and numerous investment firms. The Cyprus Investment Firms sector manages significant cross-border capital flows. CIFA (Cyprus Investment Funds Association) — the industry association representing investment fund managers.

CySEC — responsible for securities regulation and investment firm supervision. CBC (Central Bank of Cyprus) — conducts banking supervision and AML compliance monitoring. ICPAC — the professional body for accountants and auditors in Cyprus.

Βουλή των Αντιπροσώπων (House of Representatives) — unicameral parliament with 56 elected members (80 seats including 24 reserved for Turkish Cypriot community). Contact through parliament.cy. Cyprus is an membro da UE with robust democratic institutions. Moneyval (Council of Europe's Committee of Experts on the Evaluation of Anti-Money Laundering Measures) evaluates Cyprus's AML framework.

CZ.s: Central European industrial economia ($347B PIB) with strong manufacturing (19.9% PIB, automotive-dominated). Small coal sector (lignite in northern Bohemia). membro da UE. Significant nuclear energy capacity (Dukovany, Temelín). Serviços a 60.2% PIB.

sectors.w: Czechia's lignite mineração is concentrated in the Ústí nad Labem region (Sokolovská uhelná, Severní energetická). EU ETS carbon pricing makes Czech lignite uncompetitive — the interesse sobreposto between mine operators and local employment breaks when carbon costs exceed revenue.
bp: Align with EU ETS carbon pricing and eliminate free allowances for Czech lignite power. Quando plants pay the full carbon price, lignite electricity becomes more expensive than alternatives and the interesse sobreposto breaks.
Mandatory por usina emissões and health-impact reporting. Czech lignite plants cause significant air pollution — publishing health costs per MWh makes W computable.
Não é possível quebrar. Lignite combustion releases CO₂ and pollutants by physical process.
a: Report emissões violations to ČIŽP (Czech Environmental Inspectorate)
Document health impacts in mineração communities — report to regional public health authorities
Use the Czech denunciante protection law (zákon o ochraně oznamovatelů, 2023)
ČEZ (majority estatal): Accelerate coal phase-out timeline and energia renovável investment
Sev.en Energy: Desenvolver mine closure and site remediation plans
Investir em nuclear energy expansion as baseload alternative
ERÚ: Reformar electricity tariffs to reflect carbon costs
MŽP: Fortalecer air quality enforcement in mineração regions
Czech Coal Commission: Implementar recommendations on coal phase-out by 2033
Contacte o seu Poslanec or Senátor in the Parlament para exigir a binding coal phase-out timeline with just transition measures
Support EU Just Transition Fund allocation for Ústí nad Labem and Moravian-Silesian regions
Advocate for accelerated EU ETS reform
Pressionar por Territorial Just Transition Plans with concrete retraining programs
i: ČIŽP (Česká inspekce životního prostředí / Czech Environmental Inspectorate) — the enforcement agency for ambiental law, conducting inspections and imposing sanções. MŽP (Ministerstvo životního prostředí / Ministry of the Environment) — oversees ambiental policy. Zákon o ochraně oznamovatelů (Act on Protection of Whistleblowers, 2023) — Czech transposition of EU Whistleblower Directive.

ČEZ Group — Czechia's largest energy company (70% estatal), operating coal, nuclear, and renewable power plants. Sev.en Energy — the largest private lignite mineração and power company. Sokolovská uhelná — operates lignite mines in the Sokolov basin.

ERÚ (Energetický regulační úřad / Energy Regulatory Office) — the independent regulador de energia. Czech Coal Commission (Uhelná komise) — established to recommend a coal phase-out date. Recommended 2033, but implementation timelines remain debated.

Parlament České republiky — bicameral: Poslanecká sněmovna (200 members) and Senát (81 members). Contact through psp.cz and senat.cz. EU Just Transition Fund — Czechia is eligible for significant funding for coal-dependent regions.

DK.s: Advanced Nordic economia ($424.5B PIB) with global leadership in wind energy (Vestas, Ørsted). Intensive livestock agriculture (28M pigs for 5.8M people) creates significant nitrogen pollution. Serviços a 63.5% PIB. World's most ambitious climate law (70% reduction by 2030).

sectors.w: Denmark's intensive pig farming generates nitrogen pollution visible in marine dead zones (inner Danish waters, Baltic Sea). EU Nitrates Directive compliance data makes W computable from farm-level nitrogen balance sheets.
bp: Reformar agricultural subsidies to penalize nitrogen surplus per hectare. Quando the interesse sobreposto between livestock producers and feed importers is broken by internalizing the nitrogen externality, intensive livestock becomes unprofitable without subsidy.

Mandatory farm-level nitrogen and phosphorus balance disclosure. Denmark already has one of Europe's most detailed agricultural data systems — extending to mandatory public disclosure makes W computable.

Parcialmente quebrável. Intensive livestock inherently concentrates nutrients beyond ecosystem absorption. However, Denmark's leadership in biogas from manure demonstrates dependência do sistema can be reduced through circular nutrient management.
a: Report nitrogen limit violations to Miljøstyrelsen (Danish Environmental Protection Agency)
Document animal welfare violations — report to Fødevarestyrelsen
Use Danish denunciante law (Lov om beskyttelse af denunciantee, 2021)
Danish Crown, Arla: Investir em plant-based protein alternatives
Implementar nitrogen-neutral farming targets across supply chains
Adotar Scope 3 agricultural emissões reporting
Miljøministeriet: Tighten nitrogen quotas for marine ecosystem recovery
Landbrugsstyrelsen: Reformar CAP eco-scheme payments to reward nitrogen-negative farming
Implementar the 2021 agreement on green transition of Danish agriculture (55-65% emissões reduction by 2030)
Contacte o seu member of the Folketing para exigir enforcement of agricultural nitrogen reduction targets
Support EU-wide mandatory nitrogen balance disclosure
Advocate for carbon border adjustment on imported animal feed from desmatamento regions
Pressionar por Baltic Sea Action Plan implementation through HELCOM
i: Miljøstyrelsen — responsible for ambiental regulation including agricultural emissões and water quality. Fødevarestyrelsen (Danish Veterinary and Food Administration) — oversees food safety and animal welfare. Danish Whistleblower Act (2021) — transposition of EU Directive 2019/1937.
Danish Crown — one of Europe's largest meat processors (farmer cooperative). Arla Foods — multinational dairy cooperative headquartered in Denmark. DLG Group — major agricultural input cooperative.

Miljøministeriet (Ministry of Environment) — ambiental policy including agricultural regulation. Landbrugsstyrelsen (Danish Agricultural Agency) — administers CAP payments. The 2021 green agriculture agreement is one of Europe's most ambitious.

Folketinget — unicameral parliament (179 members). Contact through ft.dk. Klimaloven (Climate Act, 2020) — legally binding 70% emissões reduction target by 2030. HELCOM (Helsinki Commission) — the governing body of the Convention on the Protection of the Marine Environment of the Baltic Sea Area.

DO.s: Caribbean's largest economia ($124.3B PIB) with tourism, manufacturing (free trade zones), and mineração (2.04% PIB — Pueblo Viejo gold mine, one of the world's largest). Serviços a 59.8% PIB. Climate vulnerable — hurricanes and sea level rise.

sectors.w: Pueblo Viejo gold mine (Barrick Gold) generates cyanide and mercury contamination risks. Quando por mina water quality and health data is published alongside production revenue, W se torna computável.
bp: Mandate ambiental bonds that fully internalize mine closure and remediation costs. Quando mineração companies pre-fund cleanup, o interesse sobreposto em extraction without ambiental accountability enfraquece.
Mandatory water quality monitoring and health-impact disclosure per mine. Publishing downstream contamination data makes W computable from mineração revenue.
Parcialmente quebrável. Open-pit mineração disturbs landscapes. However, improved tailings management and water treatment can reduce dependência do sistema.
a: Report ambiental violations to the Ministry of Environment and Natural Resources
Document community health impacts — report to health authorities
Report through Barrick Gold's compliance channels
Barrick Gold (Pueblo Viejo): Implementar GISTM tailings management standards
Adotar full water quality disclosure for all operations
Investir em community development programs per mineração code requirements
Ministry of Energy and Mines: Fortalecer ambiental conditions in mineração concessions
Aplicar Mining Law 146-71 ambiental provisions
Publish mineração sector ambiental compliance data
Contacte o seu Diputado or Senador in the Congreso Nacional para exigir mandatory ambiental bonds for mineração operations
Support EITI membership for the Dominican Republic
Advogar através de SICA for regional mineração ambiental standards
Pressionar por mineração revenue transparência
i: Ministerio de Medio Ambiente y Recursos Naturales — the environment ministry. Dirección General de Minería — the mineração directorate under the Ministry of Energy and Mines. Defensor del Pueblo — the national ombudsman.
Barrick Gold — operates Pueblo Viejo, one of the world's largest gold mines (JV with Newmont). The mine produces ~800,000 ounces of gold annually.
Ministerio de Energía y Minas — oversees mineração policy. Ley 146-71 (Mining Law) — governs mineração concessions and ambiental obligations.
Congreso Nacional — bicameral: Cámara de Diputados (190 members) and Senado (32 members). The Dominican Republic is a presidential democracia.

DZ.s: Major oil and gas producer ($269.3B PIB) with 22.46% combined oil and gas rents. Sonatrach is Africa's largest company. Economy heavily dependent on hydrocarbon exports (90%+ of export revenue). Agricultura a 14% PIB (primarily wheat and dates). Authoritarian governance.

sectors.w: Algeria's oil production is declining from mature Hassi Messaoud and Hassi R'Mel fields. As domestic energy consumption grows (population of 45M+), the exportable surplus shrinks. Quando production costs exceed export revenue minus domestic consumption, o interesse sobreposto em continued expansion breaks.
bp: Redirect hydrocarbon revenues to solar energy (Algeria has the Sahara — one of Earth's highest solar irradiance zones). Quando solar electricity is cheaper than burning domestically-consumed gas, o interesse sobreposto em gas-fired power generation breaks.
Implementar por campo emissões disclosure for Sonatrach operations. Algeria's massive gas flaring (visible from satellite) makes ambiental damage measurable — publishing this data makes W computable.
Não é possível quebrar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report ambiental violations through Sonatrach internal channels
Document gas flaring data — report to Ministry of Energy and Mines
Report concerns to international partner compliance systems (Eni, TotalEnergies, Equinor)
Sonatrach: Desenvolver solar energy division using Saharan land bank
Implementar flaring reduction program
International partners (Eni, TotalEnergies): Apply global emissões standards to Algerian operations
ALNAFT: Fortalecer ambiental conditions in exploration and production contracts
CREG: Accelerate domestic energia renovável deployment (the PNER 2030 targets 22GW solar)
ARH: Aplicar flaring reduction regulations
Advogar através de OPEC for managed production decline aligned with Paris Agreement
Support Mediterranean Solar Plan and Desertec-style initiatives for Saharan solar export
Pressionar por EITI membership for Algeria
Leverage Algeria's gas supply role to Europe for energy transition dialogue
i: Sonatrach — Algeria's state oil and gas company, Africa's largest company by revenue. Sonatrach controls all upstream and downstream petroleum operations. Note: Algeria has limited press freedom and no comprehensive denunciante protection. Independent reporting is constrained.
Sonatrach operates the world's longest natural gas pipelines (Transmed to Italy, MEG to Spain). Eni, TotalEnergies, Equinor, Occidental — major international partners in Algerian upstream operations.

ALNAFT (Agence Nationale pour la Valorisation des Ressources en Hydrocarbures) — the hydrocarbons regulatory agency managing licensing. CREG (Commission de Régulation de l'Électricité et du Gaz) — the electricity and gas regulatory commission. ARH (Autorité de Régulation des Hydrocarbures) — the hydrocarbons sector regulator.

Note: Algeria is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. The Assemblée populaire nationale (407 members) and Conseil de la Nation (174 members) do not function as independent democratic institutions. International channels: OPEC membership, Mediterranean dialogue (Union for the Mediterranean), African Union, Arab League. Algeria's strategic gas supply position (supplier to Italy, Spain via pipeline) gives it leverage in energy transition negotiations.

EC.s: Oil-dependent economia ($124.7B PIB) with 6.41% oil rents. PetroEcuador manages state interests. Extraordinary biodiversity (Galápagos, Amazon basin). The 2008 Constitution grants 'rights of nature' (Pachamama). Agricultura a 9.5% PIB (bananas, shrimp, cacao).

sectors.w: Ecuador's 2023 referendum voted to ban oil extraction in Yasuní National Park (one of the world's most biodiverse areas). This democratic decision to forgo extraction directly breaks the interesse sobreposto. Extending this precedent to other sensitive areas accelerates the transition.
bp: Implementar the Yasuní referendum result (ban extraction in ITT block) and extend the precedent to other ecologically sensitive areas. Quando democratic decisions override extraction interests, the interesse sobreposto breaks through civic action.

Mandate per-block ambiental impact disclosure for all PetroEcuador and private operator fields. Ecuador's ambiental data (Amazon desmatamento, water contamination from legacy Texaco/Chevron operations) makes W measurable.
Não é possível quebrar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report ambiental violations to MAATE (Ministry of Environment, Water and Ecological Transition)
Document community health impacts from oil operations — report to the Defensoría del Pueblo
Use Ecuador's Constitutional protections for ambiental defenders
PetroEcuador: Desenvolver transition plan to phase out operations in ecologically sensitive areas
Implementar TCFD-aligned disclosure
Investir em remediation of legacy contamination (Lago Agrio)
MAATE: Aplicar Yasuní referendum result and strengthen ambiental licensing
ARCERNNR: Reformar energy regulation to accelerate renewable deployment
SHE (Secretaría de Hidrocarburos del Ecuador): Align licensing with rights of nature framework
Contacte o seu Asambleísta in the Asamblea Nacional para exigir implementation of the Yasuní referendum and extension to other sensitive areas
Leverage Ecuador's rights of nature framework in international climate negotiations
Support the Amazon Cooperation Treaty Organization (ACTO) for regional ambiental governance
Pressionar por debt-for-nature swaps to finance the transition away from oil dependence
i: MAATE (Ministerio del Ambiente, Agua y Transición Ecológica) — Ecuador's environment ministry. Defensoría del Pueblo — the national ombudsman. Ecuador's 2008 Constitution (Articles 71-74) grants legally enforceable rights to nature (Pachamama), a global first.

EP PetroEcuador — the state oil company. PetroAmazonas EP — manages upstream operations in the Amazon basin. The Lago Agrio case (Chevron/Texaco) — one of the world's largest ambiental lawsuits over oil contamination in the Ecuadorian Amazon.
ARCERNNR (Agencia de Regulación y Control de Energía y Recursos Naturales No Renovables) — the energy and natural resources regulatory agency.

Asamblea Nacional del Ecuador — unicameral parliament (137 members). Contact through asambleanacional.gob.ec. Ecuador's rights of nature framework (2008 Constitution) and the 2023 Yasuní referendum are globally significant legal precedents for ambiental governance.

EE.s: Advanced digital economia ($43.1B PIB) with unique oil shale dependence (0.96% oil rents from kukersite extraction). Services-dominant (65.7% PIB). EU and NATO member. World leader in e-governance. Oil shale power generation is Estonia's largest source of CO₂ emissões.

sectors.w: Estonia's oil shale sector is concentrated in Ida-Viru county (Eesti Energia/Enefit). EU ETS carbon costs make oil shale electricity the most expensive fossil option. Quando carbon pricing fully applies, the interesse sobreposto between shale operators and electricity consumers breaks.
bp: Full EU ETS alignment without compensation. Oil shale electricity already costs more than wind in the Baltic. Quando subsidies and free allowances are eliminated, o interesse sobreposto em oil shale generation breaks — renewables are cheaper.
Mandatory por usina lifecycle emissões and health-impact disclosure for oil shale processing. Estonia's detailed ambiental monitoring makes W computable.
Não é possível quebrar. Oil shale processing releases CO₂, particulate matter, and phenolic wastewater by physical and chemical process.
a: Report ambiental violations to Keskkonnaamet (Environmental Board)
Document health impacts in Ida-Viru communities
Use Estonian denunciante law (rikkumisest teavitaja kaitse seadus, 2022)
Eesti Energia/Enefit: Accelerate transition from oil shale to energia renovável
Investir em Ida-Viru economic diversification
Adotar binding emissões reduction targets
Konkurentsiamet: Reformar electricity tariffs to reflect full ambiental costs
Ministry of Environment: Fortalecer oil shale processing ambiental standards
Implementar just transition plan for Ida-Viru county
Contacte o seu member of the Riigikogu para exigir a binding oil shale phase-out timeline
Support EU Just Transition Fund allocation for Ida-Viru region
Advocate for Baltic energy market integration to enable energia renovável scaling
Pressionar por HELCOM enforcement on Baltic Sea pollution from oil shale waste
i: Keskkonnaamet (Environmental Board) — Estonia's ambiental enforcement and permitting authority. Keskkonnaministeerium (Ministry of the Environment) — oversees ambiental policy. Estonia transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive in 2022.

Eesti Energia (Enefit) — the estatal energy company and dominant oil shale operator. Enefit operates the Narva power plants (the largest oil shale power plants in the world) and oil shale processing facilities.

Konkurentsiamet (Competition Authority) — also serves as Estonia's regulador de energia. Ida-Viru county hosts ~90% of Estonia's oil shale operations and faces significant economic transition challenges.

Riigikogu — Estonia's unicameral parliament (101 members). Contact through riigikogu.ee. Estonia's Climate Ambition target includes significant emissões reduction from the energy sector. EU Just Transition Fund — Estonia receives JTF funding for Ida-Viru transition.

EG.s: Largest Arab economia by population ($389.1B PIB). Oil and gas rents at 5.04%. Major gas producer (Zohr field, Mediterranean). Agricultura a 13.7% PIB. High military expenditure. Hosted COP27. Suez Canal provides strategic global trade leverage.

sectors.w: Egypt's Zohr gas field (discovered 2015) transformed Egypt from gas importer to exporter. COP27 presidency created domestic pressure for emissões transparência. Per-field emissões disclosure makes W computable.
bp: Redirect gas export revenue to solar energy development. Egypt's solar irradiance is among the world's highest. Quando solar electricity undercuts gas-fired generation domestically, o interesse sobreposto em gas-for-domestic-power enfraquece.

Implementar por campo emissões disclosure for Zohr and other major fields. Egypt's COP27 commitments create institutional pressure — making lifecycle emissões visible per unit of production makes W computable.
Não é possível quebrar by policy alone. Gas combustion releases CO₂.

Report through EGPC internal compliance mechanisms

EEAA: Fortalecer ambiental standards for gas processing
Leverage Egypt's COP27 presidency legacy to strengthen national climate commitments
Advogar através de Arab League for regional emissões reduction coordination
Support Mediterranean energy transition dialogue through Union for the Mediterranean
Pressionar por Suez Canal green shipping corridor initiatives
i: EEAA (Egyptian Environmental Affairs Agency) — Egypt's ambiental regulatory body. EGPC (Egyptian General Petroleum Corporation) — the state oil and gas company. EGAS (Egyptian Natural Gas Holding Company) — manages natural gas operations. Note: Egypt has severe restrictions on sociedade civil and press freedom. Whistleblower protections are minimal.
Eni — operates Zohr, the largest gas field in the Mediterranean. BP — major operator in the West Nile Delta. EGPC and EGAS manage state interests in all petroleum operations.

Ministry of Petroleum and Mineral Resources — oversees hydrocarbons policy. NREA (New and Renewable Energy Authority) — responsible for energia renovável development. Egypt's Integrated Sustainable Energy Strategy targets 42% renewable electricity by 2035.

Note: Egypt under President el-Sisi is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. The House of Representatives (Majlis al-Nuwab, 596 members) does not function as an independent democratic institution. International channels: Arab League, African Union (Egypt hosted AU in 2019), Union for the Mediterranean, COP27 legacy initiatives.

ET.s: Africa's second-most-populous country ($149.7B PIB). Agriculture dominates (34.8% PIB, primarily coffee, teff, livestock). Rapid industrialization drive (industrial parks). Major hydropower (GERD — Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam). Post-civil war recovery.

sectors.w: Ethiopia's agricultural expansion is the primary driver of desmatamento (fr=5.6% PIB equivalent in forest rents). Quando per-farm ambiental data (tree cover, soil carbon) is linked to agricultural output, W se torna computável.
bp: Scale Ethiopia's Productive Safety Net Program (PSNP) to include ambiental conditionality. Quando agricultural support payments require soil conservation and tree planting, o interesse sobreposto em clearing forest for short-term cultivation enfraquece.

Satellite-based monitoring of forest cover and degradação do solo published at woreda level. Ethiopia's Green Legacy Initiative provides political commitment — mandatory monitoring data makes W computable.

Parcialmente quebrável. Subsistence agriculture on degraded land inherently affects soil and water systems. However, Ethiopia's traditional conservation agriculture (terracing, agroforestry) can reduce dependência do sistema.
a: Report illegal desmatamento to regional ambiental protection authorities
Document land grabbing or forced displacement — report to EHRC (Ethiopian Human Rights Commission)
Report through agricultural development agent (DA) networks at kebele level

Advogar através de African Union (headquartered in Addis Ababa) for continental agricultural sustainability standards
Pressionar por international climate finance for Ethiopia's agricultural transformation
Support Nile Basin Initiative cooperation on sustainable watershed management
i: Regional ambiental protection authorities — Ethiopia's federal system devolves ambiental management to regional states. EHRC (Ethiopian Human Rights Commission) — the national human rights institution. Desenvolverment Agents (DAs) — government agricultural extension workers deployed at kebele (ward) level throughout rural Ethiopia.

ECX (Ethiopian Commodity Exchange) — the commodities trading platform for coffee, sesame, and other agricultural products. Ethiopia is Africa's largest coffee producer and coffee is the country's largest export. Industrial parks development corporation — manages Ethiopia's growing manufacturing sector.

Ministry of Agriculture — oversees agricultural policy and the PSNP. EFCCC (Environment, Forest and Climate Change Commission) — Ethiopia's autoridade ambiental. Green Legacy Initiative — the government's flagship reforestation program targeting 20 billion seedlings.

Note: Ethiopia is a federal parlamentar republic but democratic governance has been constrained by civil conflict (Tigray war 2020-2022). The House of Peoples' Representatives (547 members) is the lower house. International channels: African Union (HQ in Addis Ababa), Nile Basin Initiative, IGAD (Intergovernmental Authority on Desenvolverment), AFR100 (Africa's contribution to the Bonn Challenge on forest restoration).

FI.s: Advanced Nordic economia ($298.7B PIB) with strong forestry sector. Serviços a 62.2% PIB, manufacturing at 14.2%. High healthcare expenditure (10.47% PIB). Major pulp and paper producers (UPM, Stora Enso, Metsä Group). membro da UE with robust ambiental regulation.

sectors.w: Finland's forestry-driven monoculture (pine/spruce plantations replacing natural forests) reduces biodiversity and degrades peatland carbon stocks. Quando per-harvest biodiversity and carbon data is published alongside timber revenue, W se torna computável.
bp: Reformar EU CAP and national forestry subsidies to reward biodiversity and carbon storage rather than timber volume. Quando subsidies incentivize continuous-cover forestry over clear-cutting, o interesse sobreposto em plantation monoculture enfraquece.

Mandatory per-harvest ambiental data (biodiversity indicators, soil carbon, water quality). Finland's Metsähallitus (state forest enterprise) already collects extensive data — making it publicly accessible makes W computable.

Parcialmente quebrável. Timber plantation inherently reduces biodiversity compared to natural forest. However, continuous-cover forestry and extended rotation lengths can significantly reduce dependência do sistema.

Document biodiversity loss in logged areas — report to SYKE (Finnish Environment Institute)

i: ELY-keskus — regional authorities responsible for ambiental monitoring and enforcement. SYKE (Suomen ympäristökeskus / Finnish Environment Institute) — the national ambiental research and monitoring institute. Ilmoittajansuojelulaki (Whistleblower Protection Act, 2023) — Finnish transposition of EU Whistleblower Directive.

UPM-Kymmene — one of Finland's largest forestry companies. Stora Enso — Finnish-Swedish forestry and paper company. Metsä Group — cooperative of 100,000+ Finnish forest owners. Metsähallitus — state enterprise managing one-third of Finland's land area.

MMM (Maa- ja metsätalousministeriö) — the ministry responsible for agriculture and forestry policy. National Forest Strategy 2025 — Finland's strategic framework for forestry sector development. EU Nature Restoration Law — requires restoration of degraded ecosystems including forests.

Eduskunta (Parliament of Finland) — unicameral legislature with 200 members. Contact through eduskunta.fi. The Maa- ja metsätalousvaliokunta (Agriculture and Forestry Committee) handles forestry legislation. Finland's climate law targets carbon neutrality by 2035.

GA.s: Oil-dependent Central African economia ($20.9B PIB) with 15.83% combined oil and gas rents. 88% forest cover — one of the world's most forested countries. Low population (2.4M). Military coup August 2023 ended the Bongo family's 55-year rule.

sectors.w: Gabon's oil production is in terminal decline from mature fields (peaked at ~370,000 bpd in 1997, now ~200,000). As production falls, the interesse sobreposto between operators and government revenue enfraquece. Gabon's forests offer alternative revenue through carbon credits.
bp: Monetize Gabon's forest carbon stocks through REDD+ and voluntary carbon markets. Gabon received $17M from Norway for forest protection in 2021 — scaling this replaces declining oil revenue and breaks o interesse sobreposto em extraction.
Implementar por campo emissões disclosure for all operators (TotalEnergies, Perenco, Assala Energy). Making ambiental costs visible makes W computable.
Não é possível quebrar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂.

Document oil spills or contamination — report through operator compliance channels
Reportar a EITI Gabon

i: ANPN (Agence Nationale des Parcs Nationaux) — manages Gabon's 13 national parks (covering 11% of territory). GOC (Gabon Oil Company) — the state oil company. EITI Gabon — member since 2004. Note: Gabon is under military transition government since August 2023 coup.
TotalEnergies — the largest oil producer in Gabon. Perenco, Assala Energy — other major operators. GOC (Gabon Oil Company) — the state oil company.
Ministry of Hydrocarbons — oversees oil and gas policy. DGEPN (Direction Générale de l'Environnement et de la Protection de la Nature) — ambiental directorate.

Note: Gabon is under military transition government (Comité pour la Transition et la Restauration des Institutions, CTRI) since August 2023. Democratic institutions are suspended. International channels: CEMAC, CAFI, African Union, OPEC membership. Gabon's 2021 deal with Norway's sovereign wealth fund for forest protection is a model for forest-state climate finance.

GE.s: Small South Caucasus economia ($34.2B PIB) with mineração sector (1.28% PIB, primarily manganese and copper). Services-dominant (62% PIB). candidato à UE country (2023). Significant hydropower potential. Political tensions between pro-EU and pro-Russia orientations.

sectors.w: Georgia's manganese mineração (Chiatura) and copper mineração generate significant water pollution. EU accession ambiental alignment will require improved monitoring. Quando water quality data is linked to mineração output, W se torna computável.
bp: EU accession ambiental alignment. Quando Georgia must meet EU ambiental standards for mineração, o interesse sobreposto em low-cost extraction without ambiental controls enfraquece.
Mandatory por mina water quality and emissões disclosure. Georgia's National Environmental Agency collects some data — extending to public disclosure makes W computable.
Parcialmente quebrável. Mining disturbs landscapes, but improved tailings management can reduce dependência do sistema.

i: NEA (National Environmental Agency) — responsible for ambiental monitoring, permitting, and enforcement. Ministry of Environmental Protection and Agriculture — oversees ambiental and agricultural policy. Georgia's Law on Freedom of Speech and Expression provides some protections for reporting.

Georgian Manganese — subsidiary of ERG (Eurasian Resources Group), operating the Chiatura manganese mines. RMG Copper — operates copper mines. Georgia's mineração sector is primarily controlled by international investors.
National Agency of Mines — responsible for mineração licensing and oversight. Georgia's EU Association Agreement (2014) includes provisions for ambiental alignment.

Sakartvelos Parlamenti (Parliament of Georgia) — unicameral legislature with 150 members. Contact through parliament.ge. Georgia is an candidato à UE country (2023) and aspirant to full membro da UEship. EU Association Agreement — includes Deep and Comprehensive Free Trade Area (DCFTA) with ambiental provisions.

GH.s: West African economia ($82.3B PIB) with significant oil (4.06% rents), gold mineração (5.17% mineral rents), and cocoa agriculture (20.9% ag share). One of Africa's most stable democracies. Debt crisis (2022 IMF bailout). Illegal artisanal gold mineração (galamsey) is a major ambiental crisis.

sectors.w: Ghana's galamsey (illegal artisanal mineração) crisis has contaminated water bodies across cocoa-growing regions. Quando por mina water quality data is published and linked to gold production, W se torna computável. The galamsey problem is so severe that it threatens Ghana's water supply.
bp: Formalize artisanal mineração with ambiental standards and alternative livelihood programs. Quando legal small-scale mineração provides comparable income with less ambiental damage, o interesse sobreposto em illegal extraction enfraquece.
Satellite-based water quality monitoring linked to mineração areas. Ghana's Water Resources Commission already monitors water quality — extending to real-time public data makes W computable.
Parcialmente quebrável. Mining disturbs landscapes. However, formalized artisanal mineração with mercury-free processing (e.g., gravity separation) can significantly reduce dependência do sistema.

i: IMCIM (Inter-Ministerial Committee on Illegal Mining) — established to coordinate government response to the galamsey crisis. WRC (Water Resources Commission) — manages Ghana's water resources and monitors water quality. Minerals Commission — the regulatory body for the mineração sector, responsible for licensing and compliance monitoring.

Gold Fields — operates Tarkwa and Damang mines. Newmont — operates Ahafo and Akyem mines. AngloGold Ashanti — major gold mineração company with Ghanaian operations. Ghana is Africa's largest gold producer.

EPA (Environmental Protection Agency of Ghana) — responsible for ambiental permitting, monitoring, and enforcement. Forestry Commission — manages Ghana's forest reserves, many of which are threatened by illegal mineração. EITI Ghana — Ghana has been EITI compliant since 2010.

Parliament of Ghana — unicameral legislature with 275 members. Contact through parliament.gh. Ghana is a presidential democracia with peaceful transfers of power. Minamata Convention on Mercury — Ghana has ratified this treaty requiring phase-down of mercury use in artisanal gold mineração.

GN.s: Resource-rich West African economia ($25B PIB) with massive bauxite reserves (world's largest — 7.4B tons). Agricultura a 31% PIB. Military junta since 2021 coup. Guinea supplies ~25% of global bauxite (the ore used to produce aluminum).

sectors.w: Guinea's bauxite mineração displaces communities and contaminates water sources. As aluminum buyers face ESG pressure (EU CSRD, Apple/Tesla supply chain requirements), making por mina ambiental data visible breaks system independence.
bp: International supply chain due diligence (EU CSDDD) requiring ambiental and human rights compliance for bauxite. Quando buyers must verify mine-level conditions, o interesse sobreposto em low-cost, unregulated extraction enfraquece.
Per-mine ambiental and community impact disclosure through EITI and supply chain traceability. Quando downstream manufacturers can see the welfare cost per ton of bauxite, W se torna computável.
Parcialmente quebrável. Open-pit bauxite mineração inherently disturbs landscapes. However, progressive rehabilitation and dust suppression can reduce dependência do sistema.

Ministry of Mines: Aplicar 2011 Mining Code ambiental provisions
CPDM (Centre de Promotion et de Développement Minier): Fortalecer ambiental monitoring capacity
EITI Guinea: Expand reporting to ambiental and community data

Support EU CSDDD implementation requiring due diligence for bauxite imports from Guinea

Support international accountability mechanisms for mineração-affected communities
i: Note: Guinea is under military rule (CNRD — Comité National du Rassemblement et du Développement) since September 2021. Independent sociedade civil operates under severe constraints. Bureau Guinéen des Études et Évaluations Environnementales (BGEEE) — ambiental assessment authority. International monitoring: Human Rights Watch and Inclusive Desenvolverment International have documented impacts of bauxite mineração on Guinean communities.

CBG (Compagnie des Bauxites de Guinée) — JV of Alcoa, Rio Tinto, and Dadco, one of the world's largest bauxite producers. SMB (Société Minière de Boké) — Chinese-backed consortium (includes Winning International, Shandong Weiqiao). GAC (Guinea Alumina Corporation) — Emirates Global Aluminium subsidiary. ASI (Aluminium Stewardship Initiative) — the industry sustainability standard for the aluminum value chain.

Ministère des Mines et de la Géologie — oversees mineração policy and licensing. Code Minier (2011, revised 2013) — Guinea's mineração code establishing ambiental obligations. EITI Guinea — Guinea has been EITI compliant, publishing reports on mineração revenues.

Note: Guinea's democratic institutions are suspended under military rule. The Conseil National de la Transition (CNT) serves as a transitional legislature. International channels: ECOWAS (Guinea suspended), African Union, international mineração company home-country oversight (UK, Canada, UAE, China). EU Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive (CSDDD) — will require EU companies to address human rights and ambiental impacts in their supply chains.

GQ.s: Small petrostate ($12.8B PIB) with 21.57% combined oil and gas rents. One of Africa's highest per-capita PIBs but extreme inequality. Authoritarian rule (Obiang family since 1979 — world's longest-serving head of state). Oil production declining from mature fields.

sectors.w: Equatorial Guinea's oil production has declined ~75% from peak. As fields deplete, the interesse sobreposto between operators and government revenue enfraquece. Without new discoveries, the economic case for diversification becomes unavoidable.
bp: Economic diversification as oil depletes. Quando production revenue falls below government operating costs, o interesse sobreposto em continued extraction from depleting fields breaks — alternative revenue sources become necessary.
Per-field emissões disclosure through international operator reporting (ExxonMobil, Marathon Oil). Making ambiental costs visible makes W computable.
Não é possível quebrar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂.

i: Note: Equatorial Guinea is one of the world's most autoritário states. No independent media, sociedade civil, or labor organizations exist. EG LNG (Equatorial Guinea LNG Holdings) — operates the LNG processing plant. International engagement: US Department of Justice Kleptocracy Asset Recovery Initiative has investigated Obiang family assets. Transparency International consistently ranks Equatorial Guinea among the most corrupt countries.
ExxonMobil — the largest oil operator in Equatorial Guinea. Marathon Oil — operates in offshore blocks. GEPetrol — the state national oil company.

Ministry of Mines and Hydrocarbons — oversees oil and gas operations. Note: Equatorial Guinea was suspended from EITI in 2010 for insufficient progress on transparência. BEAC (central bank of CEMAC zone) provides limited macroeconomic oversight.

Note: Equatorial Guinea under President Obiang (in power since 1979) has no democratic institutions. The Cámara de Representantes del Pueblo and Senate are rubber-stamp bodies. International channels: CEMAC, African Union, UN Human Rights Council. Several countries (US, France, Switzerland) have pursued legal action against Obiang family members for corruption and money laundering.

GR.s: EU economia ($256.2B PIB) with dominant services sector (68% PIB, tourism-heavy). Low resource extraction. Post-debt-crisis recovery. Major shipping industry (Greek-owned fleet is the world's largest). Agricultura a 3.8% PIB (olives, wine).

sectors.w: Greece's Mediterranean diet is being displaced by UPF consumption, contributing to rising obesity (Greece has one of Europe's highest childhood obesity rates). Quando diet-related healthcare costs are published per product category through EOPYY health insurance data, W se torna computável.
bp: Implementar front-of-pack health labelling to make welfare costs visible to consumers. Quando UPF health costs are visible at point of sale, the interesse sobreposto between processors and retailers in high-margin UPF enfraquece.

EOPYY (National Organization for the Provision of Healthcare Services) publication of diet-attributable disease costs per food category. Greece's national health system generates data to make W computable.
Not fully breakable. Ultra-processing alters food matrix. Reformarulation mandates can reduce dependência do sistema.

Pressionar por WHO-aligned UPF marketing restrictions
i: EFET (ΕΦΕΤ — Ενιαίος Φορέας Ελέγχου Τροφίμων / Hellenic Food Authority) — Greece's food safety authority. Greece transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive in 2022.

SEVT (Σύνδεσμος Ελληνικών Βιομηχανιών Τροφίμων / Federation of Hellenic Food Industries) — the food industry trade association. Greece's traditional food sector (olive oil, dairy, honey) provides a competitive alternative to UPF.

Ministry of Health — oversees health policy. EOPYY (Εθνικός Οργανισμός Παροχής Υπηρεσιών Υγείας / National Organization for the Provision of Healthcare Services) — the national health insurer managing healthcare expenditure data.
Βουλή των Ελλήνων (Hellenic Parliament) — unicameral legislature with 300 members. Contact through hellenicparliament.gr. Greece is a parlamentar democracia and membro da UE.

GT.s: Central American economia ($113.2B PIB) with agriculture at 9.8% PIB (coffee, sugar, palm oil). Mining at 1.12% PIB (gold, silver — Marlin mine controversy). High inequality and indigenous population (40%+). Remittances significant (~18% of PIB).

sectors.w: Guatemala's palm oil expansion into Petén and Alta Verapaz drives desmatamento and displaces indigenous communities. Quando per-farm desmatamento data is linked to palm oil exports (EUDR compliance), W se torna computável.
bp: EU Deforestation Regulation compliance requiring farm-level traceability. Quando palm oil exports must prove zero-desmatamento origin, o interesse sobreposto em clearing forest for plantations breaks.

Mandatory per-farm ambiental data (tree cover, water use, pesticide application). Guatemala's CONAP (National Council for Protected Areas) can provide monitoring data — linking to agricultural output makes W computable.
Parcialmente quebrável. Palm oil monoculture reduces biodiversity. However, agroforestry integration and RSPO certification can reduce dependência do sistema.

i: CONAP (Consejo Nacional de Áreas Protegidas) — manages Guatemala's protected areas. INAB (Instituto Nacional de Bosques) — the national forest institute. PDH (Procuraduría de los Derechos Humanos) — the national human rights ombudsman. Guatemala has significant challenges with ambiental defender safety.

REPSA (Reforestadora de Palma del Petén) — major palm oil producer. GREPALMA (Gremial de Palmicultores de Guatemala) — the palm oil producers' association. RSPO (Roundtable on Sustainable Óleo de Palma) — the international certification standard.
MARN (Ministerio de Ambiente y Recursos Naturales) — Guatemala's environment ministry. MAGA (Ministerio de Agricultura, Ganadería y Alimentación) — the agriculture ministry.

Congreso de la República de Guatemala — unicameral legislature with 160 members. Guatemala has ratified ILO Convention 169 on indigenous peoples' rights. The Inter-American Court of Human Rights has issued rulings on indigenous land rights in Guatemala.

GY.s: Fastest-growing economia globally ($24.7B PIB, up from $5.5B in 2019) due to massive offshore oil discoveries (Stabroek block). Rendas do petróleo at 22.08%, mineração at 9.38% (gold, bauxite). Small population (800K). At risk of 'resource curse' — extraordinary PST exposure from rapid petro-development.

sectors.w: Guyana's 2016 production sharing agreement with ExxonMobil has been widely criticized for giving the government only 2% royalty + 50% profit oil (after cost recovery). Quando the full fiscal terms and ambiental costs are disclosed and understood, W se torna computável from production data.
bp: Renegotiate the Stabroek PSA fiscal terms to capture a fair share of petroleum wealth. Quando Guyana's government share approaches international norms (60-80% government take), o interesse sobreposto em an extractive-favorable contract enfraquece.
Mandatory por campo emissões disclosure and ambiental monitoring for all Stabroek block operations. ExxonMobil already reports global emissões — extending to por campo Guyana data makes W computable.
Não é possível quebrar by policy alone. Offshore oil production and combustion release CO₂.

Document compliance gaps in Stabroek operations — report through ExxonMobil compliance channels

Adotar zero-flaring targets
Investir em oil spill response capacity (current capacity is minimal for deepwater operations)

Ministry of Natural Resources: Commission independent review of Stabroek PSA terms
Natural Resource Fund Board: Implementar transparent investment and withdrawal rules
Contacte o seu Member of Parliament in the National Assembly para exigir independent review of Stabroek PSA fiscal terms
Support establishment of a properly resourced Petroleum Commission as independent regulator
Advocate for Natural Resource Fund transparência aligned with Santiago Principles for sovereign wealth funds

i: EPA (Environmental Protection Agency of Guyana) — responsible for ambiental permitting and monitoring of oil and gas operations. Ministry of Natural Resources — oversees petroleum policy. Audit Office of Guyana — conducts financial audits of government revenue management. Note: Guyana's regulatory capacity is severely under-resourced relative to the scale of oil development.

ExxonMobil (45%), Hess (30%), CNOOC (25%) — the Stabroek block partnership. Production began in 2019 and is expected to reach 1.2 million bpd by 2027. The Stabroek Production Sharing Agreement (2016) — governs fiscal terms. The Natural Resource Fund — Guyana's sovereign wealth fund established in 2019 to manage oil revenues.
Natural Resource Fund Act (2021) — governs the sovereign wealth fund. The Petroleum Commission (planned) — intended to serve as the independent petroleum regulator, but not yet fully established.

National Assembly of Guyana — unicameral legislature with 65 members. Guyana is a cooperative republic with Westminster-style parlamentar democracia. The Natural Resource Governance Institute (NRGI) provides technical assistance on petroleum governance. CARICOM — provides regional coordination.

HK.s: Global financial center ($406.9B PIB) with 91.8% services. Major banking and securities hub. Under Chinese sovereignty since 1997 with eroding autonomy. Significant financial intermediation — Hong Kong Stock Exchange is 5th-largest globally by market cap.

sectors.w: Hong Kong intermediates massive capital flows including fossil fuel financing. Quando Scope 3 financed emissões are disclosed per financial institution, W se torna computável from financial transaction data.
bp: Mandatory financed emissões disclosure for all HKEX-listed financial institutions. Quando banks must disclose the emissões their lending supports, o interesse sobreposto em fossil fuel financing enfraquece — ESG-aware investors reallocate.
HKMA Green and Sustainable Finance Cross-Agency Steering Group implementation. Making climate risk visible in financial products makes W computable.
Parcialmente quebrável. Financial intermediation can relocate. But Hong Kong's infrastructure and China market access create switching costs.

i: SFC (Securities and Futures Commission) — regulates securities, futures markets, and listed companies. HKEX (Hong Kong Exchanges and Clearing) — operates the stock exchange and sets listing rules including ESG disclosure requirements. HKMA (Hong Kong Monetary Authority) — the de facto central bank. Note: Under the National Security Law (2020), civil liberties are constrained.

Major banks in Hong Kong: HSBC, Standard Chartered, Bank of China (Hong Kong), Hang Seng Bank. Green and Sustainable Finance Cross-Agency Steering Group — coordinates climate finance policy across HKMA, SFC, and other regulators.
HKMA — supervises 160+ licensed banks. The HKMA's Climate Risk Management Supervisory Policy Manual (2021) sets expectations for banks' climate risk management.

Note: Hong Kong's Legislative Council (90 members) is not freely elected under the 2021 electoral reform. International engagement operates through financial regulatory cooperation (IOSCO, BIS, NGFS) and Hong Kong's role as an international financial center.

HN.s: Lower-middle-income economia ($37.1B PIB) with agriculture at 11.2% PIB (coffee, palm oil, bananas). Manufatura a 14.9% (maquiladora sector). Climate-vulnerable — Hurricane Mitch (1998) and Eta/Iota (2020). High emigration. Environmental defenders face severe threats.

sectors.w: Honduras's palm oil expansion drives desmatamento in the Aguán Valley and Mosquitia region. EUDR compliance will require farm-level traceability — when desmatamento is linked to specific supply chains, W se torna computável.
bp: EUDR compliance requiring zero-desmatamento traceability for palm oil exports. Quando buyers must verify origin, o interesse sobreposto em forest conversion for plantations breaks.
Satellite-based desmatamento monitoring linked to agricultural export data. ICF (Forest Conservation Institute) data makes W computable.
Parcialmente quebrável. Palm oil monoculture inherently reduces biodiversity. RSPO certification and agroforestry can reduce dependência do sistema.
